description: Crypto narrative archetypes -> price impact, for backtesting. Each category has archetypes; each archetype has a mechanism, directional bias, backtest_features, analog_mechanisms, and historical_instances with quantified price_impacts.

categories.summary: AI/agent tokens are a high-beta crypto narrative where a small piece of news (an AI agent posting on X, a16z association, a launchpad "graduation", a new Bittensor subnet) triggers explosive, reflexive parabolic moves followed by equally violent collapses. The sector rose from near-zero to roughly $17B-$20B aggregate market cap by mid-January 2025 (figure varies by data provider), then collapsed toward the $5B-$7B range within weeks as the same narrative liquidity unwound. It moves as a tight cohort: a flagship pump (GOAT, ai16z, VIRTUAL) drags every other AI-themed token up, and a flagship crash or macro AI shock (DeepSeek) drags them all down together.

archetypes.name: Flagship AI-Agent Token Launch Pump (reflexive parabola)
archetypes.mechanism: A novel AI-agent token launches via fair-launch/launchpad (pump.fun, Virtuals bonding curve) with a viral story (an autonomous AI, an a16z-themed DAO, a Marc Andreessen association). Early holders and bonding-curve buyers are on the winning side; the losing side is late retail FOMO chasing 50-100x narratives at the top. Price is reflexive: rising price = more attention = more buyers, with thin float and no fundamental anchor, so moves overshoot massively in both directions.
archetypes.typical_lag: immediate to hours; parabola plays out over days to ~2 weeks
archetypes.typical_duration: 1-3 weeks of run-up, then weeks-to-months of decay
archetypes.typical_magnitude_pct: +500% to +8000% on the launch token over its first 1-4 weeks; co-movement +20% to +100% on sector peers
archetypes.recurrence: Clustered Oct 2024 - Jan 2025; recurs whenever a new flagship narrative coin appears
leading_signals: viral X/Twitter mentions of an AI agent
association/endorsement by a known VC/founder (a16z, Andreessen)
pump.fun coin crossing $100M then higher mcap
Virtuals bonding curve nearing 42,000 VIRTUAL graduation
rapid holder-count growth
exchange listing announcements (Binance/Coinbase)

historical_instances.trigger: Goatseus Maximus (GOAT) launched on pump.fun, tied to the AI 'Truth Terminal' bot; went viral as an early AI-agent memecoin
historical_instances.description: GOAT launched at a fraction of a cent and ran several thousand percent within about a week as it went viral. Peak market cap in October 2024 was reported in the ~$400M-$700M range (Binance cited >$400M, CoinSwitch >$700M); claims that it was 'the first pump.fun coin to exceed $1B market cap' are NOT supported by available sources and have been removed. GOAT helped kick off the AI-agent narrative.
historical_instances.volume_or_flow_change: Explosive pump.fun volume; one of the largest pump.fun coins by mcap in late 2024

historical_instances.trigger: ai16z token launched as an AI-run investment DAO ('Marc AIndreessen'); boosted mid-Nov by a16z-related attention on X
historical_instances.description: AI16Z launched in late October at fractions of a cent, rallied through November-December, and hit an ATH of ~$2.47-$2.48 on Jan 1-2, 2025. The exact launch-day price and percentage-from-launch figure (the prior '+50000%+' claim) could not be corroborated to that precision, so it has been downgraded; the ATH and its date are well supported.
historical_instances.volume_or_flow_change: Whale accumulation; reports of a top trader realizing tens of millions of dollars

historical_instances.trigger: VIRTUAL (Virtuals Protocol, the AI-agent launchpad on Base) rallied as its agent ecosystem (AIXBT, LUNA, GAME) went viral
historical_instances.description: VIRTUAL rose strongly into late 2024, hitting an ATH in the ~$3.71-$4.50 range around late December 2024 (sources conflict on the exact peak; CoinGecko-aligned figures cite ~$3.71, others ~$4.12-$4.50) with peak market cap reported above $1.6B. Acted as the index/beta for the Base AI-agent ecosystem.
historical_instances.volume_or_flow_change: Ecosystem grew to thousands of agents; several agents reaching $130M-$300M+ mcap

historical_instances.trigger: Trump inauguration / peak risk-on sentiment lifted AI-agent tokens to sector-wide highs
historical_instances.description: AIXBT, VIRTUAL and AI16Z all printed local/all-time highs into the Jan 20 Trump inauguration as the AI-agent sector aggregate mcap peaked. Peak aggregate figures cited by sources range from ~$17B to ~$20B depending on provider and token set.
historical_instances.volume_or_flow_change: Peak speculative inflows before the Q1 collapse

archetypes.name: AI-Sector Rotation + Reflexive Crash (correlated cohort unwind)
archetypes.mechanism: AI-agent tokens trade as a single high-beta narrative basket. Capital rotates in together on hype and out together on shock. On the way up, latecomers buy the basket as a theme; on the way down, profit-takers ('smart money') dump first and trigger reflexive deleveraging across all names, because thin liquidity and shared holders mean one sell cascade hits the whole cohort. Late retail holding the basket at the top are the losers; early whales and macro-aware sellers win.
archetypes.typical_lag: immediate (hours) on the shock; cascade over days
archetypes.typical_duration: Drawdown unwinds over weeks; sector spent months below peak
archetypes.typical_magnitude_pct: -60% to -90% peak-to-trough on individual AI-agent tokens within ~6 weeks; sector mcap fell roughly -65% to -70% (from ~$17B-$20B toward $5B-$7B)
archetypes.recurrence: The full cohort crash was a single Q1-2025 regime change; smaller correlated drawdowns recur on any AI macro shock
leading_signals: smart-money / top-PnL wallets distributing
sector aggregate mcap stalling after parabola
negative AI macro headline (DeepSeek)
declining new launches / launchpad volume
funding rates flipping / OI unwind
BTC dominance rising vs alts

historical_instances.trigger: DeepSeek R1 shock: cheap Chinese open-source AI model triggered a global AI/tech selloff (Nvidia ~-17%) that spilled into crypto AI tokens
historical_instances.description: On Jan 27, 2025 the DeepSeek efficiency shock drove a sharp global AI/tech selloff (Nvidia fell ~17%) and hit AI tokens hardest, marking the top of the AI-agent narrative and the start of the cohort unwind. The specific '-$300B in a day' total-crypto-mcap figure could not be corroborated and has been downgraded to low confidence.
historical_instances.volume_or_flow_change: Risk-off; AI-token-specific selloff sharper than broad crypto

historical_instances.trigger: Smart-money profit leaders dumped AI-agent positions as the narrative cooled; sector-wide drawdown accelerated
historical_instances.description: By Feb 21, 2025 AI16Z and AIXBT were down sharply over the prior 30 days (reported ~60% and ~72% respectively), and AI16Z had corrected on the order of ~90% from its early-January high. Reports describe the top ai16z trader selling nearly his entire position.
historical_instances.volume_or_flow_change: Profit-leader distribution; sector aggregate mcap fell from its January peak toward the $5B-$7B range

historical_instances.trigger: Broad AI-agent narrative deflation; top platforms shed market cap and new agent launches collapsed
historical_instances.description: AI-agent tokens were down as much as ~90% from 2024 highs; top platforms ai16z and Virtuals shed roughly 75-90% of market cap since January. New agent launches on Virtuals dried up alongside the token drawdown.
historical_instances.volume_or_flow_change: New agent launch count collapsed; launchpad volume fell sharply

historical_instances.trigger: Continued cohort drift lower in mid-2025; AI-agent tokens still bleeding well below their Q1 peaks
historical_instances.description: Measured on CoinGecko: over Jun 4 - Jul 1, 2025 AI16Z fell 41.71% (max intra-window drawdown 48.4%), AIXBT fell 33.76% (max drawdown 44.3%), and VIRTUAL fell 21.42% (Jun 4-20, max drawdown 32.9%) - confirming the cohort still moved together and stayed in a downtrend long after the peak.
historical_instances.volume_or_flow_change: Persistent net outflows; cohort cross-correlation remained high

archetypes.name: Launchpad Graduation Pumps & AI-Infra Token Consolidation
archetypes.mechanism: Two structural sub-patterns. (a) Launchpad graduation: on Virtuals, an agent's bonding curve filling 42,000 VIRTUAL triggers a Uniswap listing - a deterministic supply/liquidity event that pumps both the new agent token and VIRTUAL (the required base currency), as buyers must acquire VIRTUAL to participate. (b) Infra consolidation: established AI-infra tokens (FET, AGIX, OCEAN) merge into a single asset (ASI), restricting supply across three tickers into one and re-rating on the combined narrative. Losers are those who ignore the deterministic mechanics; winners front-run graduations and merger conversions.
archetypes.typical_lag: immediate on graduation; days-to-weeks on merger re-rating
archetypes.typical_duration: Graduation pumps fade within days; merger re-rating persists weeks-to-months
archetypes.typical_magnitude_pct: Agent graduation: token +50% to +1000% on listing; VIRTUAL +10% to +30% on graduation waves; merger: +50% to +300% on announcement (FET ATH ~$3.47)
archetypes.recurrence: Graduations were near-daily on Virtuals during the Nov 2024 - Jan 2025 boom; mergers are rare one-offs
leading_signals: Virtuals bonding curve approaching 42,000 VIRTUAL
pending Uniswap liquidity deployment
merger/consolidation announcement
token conversion-ratio disclosure
exchange support announcements for the new ticker

historical_instances.trigger: Announcement of the Fetch.ai / SingularityNET / Ocean Protocol merger into the Artificial Superintelligence Alliance (ASI)
historical_instances.description: The ASI merger was announced on March 28, 2024. Around that period FET hit an all-time high of ~$3.47 (March 28, 2024; the prior '$3.49' was slightly overstated - better-supported figure is $3.47, with some feeds at $3.45-$3.48); AGIX and OCEAN also surged as the market priced the consolidation of three AI-infra tokens into one ASI ticker.
historical_instances.volume_or_flow_change: Re-rating on supply consolidation across 3 tickers into ASI

historical_instances.trigger: ASI token merger went live (AGIX->ASI at ~0.4333, OCEAN->ASI at ~0.4332, 1 FET = 1 ASI); migration of AGIX/OCEAN into FET/ASI
historical_instances.description: Merger migration began July 1, 2024 (Phase 1, 50%+ migrated), restricting supply into a single asset. Subsequent claims about a ~$9.2B ASI market cap and a precise '+22.7% post-merger' figure could not be corroborated and have been downgraded.
historical_instances.volume_or_flow_change: Three-token supply consolidated into one; cross-chain bridge flows

historical_instances.trigger: AIXBT and other flagship agents launched/graduated on the Virtuals bonding curve, driving demand for VIRTUAL as the mandatory base currency
historical_instances.description: AIXBT (a leading Virtuals agent, launched Nov 2024) and peers graduated via the 42,000-VIRTUAL bonding curve onto Uniswap; the graduation mechanism forced buyers to acquire VIRTUAL, contributing to VIRTUAL's strong Q4 run. The specific AIXBT ATH (~$0.54) and VIRTUAL '+850%' figures are approximate and sourced to crypto media rather than measured.
historical_instances.volume_or_flow_change: Graduation-driven VIRTUAL demand; agents reaching $130M-$300M+ mcap

archetypes.name: Idiosyncratic Agent/Protocol Events (hacks, subnet launches, dTAO)
archetypes.mechanism: Single-name catalysts specific to an agent or protocol. (a) Agent compromise/hack: an autonomous agent's wallet or dashboard is exploited, breaking the 'autonomous AI' trust thesis and triggering a sharp single-name drop. (b) Bittensor dTAO subnet launches: under Dynamic TAO (live Feb 13, 2025) each subnet gets its own Alpha token with a liquidity pool; new subnet/alpha tokens typically spike on launch then dip, and alpha-token prices directly route TAO emissions. Losers are holders trusting the agent's security or chasing fresh subnet alphas at launch; winners are exploiters and emission-aware stakers who rotate.
archetypes.typical_lag: immediate (hours) on hacks; launch-day spike then multi-day dip on subnet alphas
archetypes.typical_duration: Hack drops partly recover in days; subnet-launch dips persist until emissions re-equilibrate
archetypes.typical_magnitude_pct: Hack: -15% to -22% single-name in 24h; subnet alpha launch: initial spike then -30% to -70% dip; TAO sector beta
archetypes.recurrence: Hacks are sporadic; dTAO subnet alpha launches recur continuously since Feb 2025
leading_signals: unusual agent wallet activity / dashboard access
deleted-account or anomalous agent posts
new Bittensor subnet registration
alpha-token liquidity-pool creation
TAO staking redistribution toward a new subnet

historical_instances.trigger: Bittensor's Templar subnet completes Covenant-72B - the largest-ever decentralized LLM pre-training run; TAO rallies.
historical_instances.description: Bittensor Subnet 3 (Templar) trained Covenant-72B (72B params, 70+ independent contributors on commodity GPUs over home internet), scoring 67.1 on MMLU (~Meta Llama-2-70B class) - a proof-of-concept that decentralized AI training works. TAO rallied +66.5% in March 2026 ($183->$305 close-to-close; intra-month peak ~$371, +114% from the low), amplified by a Grayscale TAO ETF filing; the Templar subnet token jumped +194% in 7 days and the Bittensor ecosystem mcap hit ~$1.5B. A subnet/model-achievement idiosyncratic rally and the flagship of the broader DeAI sector (RENDER, Fetch/ASI, GRASS, Ocean). NOTE: high-beta and mean-reverting - TAO then fell ~35% from its May high into the June-2026 risk-off, and NEAR's AI-DeFi adoption (5M daily tx, Apr 2026) is an adoption metric, not a price catalyst.
historical_instances.volume_or_flow_change: Bittensor ecosystem mcap ~$1.5B; Grayscale TAO ETF filing; Yuma staked ~19% of supply.

historical_instances.trigger: TAO traded with the broad AI/alt downtrend in mid-2025, with high intra-window volatility around subnet/emission dynamics
historical_instances.description: Measured on CoinGecko: TAO fell 13.43% from Jun 4 to Jun 30, 2025 ($387.19 to $335.18) but with a 33.12% max intra-window drawdown and ~24% max run-up - reflecting the high volatility of the dTAO emission/subnet regime even in a flagship AI-infra token.
historical_instances.volume_or_flow_change: High intra-window volatility tied to subnet emission rotation

historical_instances.trigger: AIXBT agent's autonomous dashboard was accessed by a hacker who queued malicious replies and drained ~55.5 ETH (~$100K) from a Simulacrum wallet
historical_instances.description: The hack broke the 'secure autonomous agent' thesis; the AIXBT token fell roughly 16-22% in 24h despite the team insisting fundamentals were unchanged. The incident is dated March 18, 2025 (some coverage references March 19); the prior catalog date (March 19) has been corrected.
historical_instances.volume_or_flow_change: Single-name selloff; limited contagion to broader cohort

historical_instances.trigger: Bittensor Dynamic TAO (dTAO) upgrade went live, giving every subnet its own Alpha token and liquidity pool; market staking now routes TAO emissions
historical_instances.description: dTAO launched Feb 13, 2025. New subnet alpha tokens characteristically surge at launch then dip as the market re-prices them; alpha prices directly determine each subnet's share of daily TAO emissions. The '~$1.5B cumulative subnet-token valuation by March 2026' figure is forward/uncorroborated and downgraded.
historical_instances.volume_or_flow_change: Open-market staking redirects emissions; recurring launch-and-dip flow per subnet

categories.summary: Bitcoin dominance (BTC.D = BTC market cap / total crypto market cap) is the master regime gauge for capital rotation *within* crypto. When dominance rises, capital concentrates in Bitcoin — typically early-bull accumulation, ETF-driven legs, or risk-off flight-to-quality. When dominance falls, capital rotates down the risk curve into altcoins ('alt-season'), historically a late-cycle, risk-on, euphoria signal. The Altcoin Season Index (share of top-50 alts beating BTC over 90 days) operationalizes it: >75 = alt-season, <25 = Bitcoin season. A backtester should treat dominance as a regime filter, not a directional bet on total market cap.

archetypes.name: BTC dominance regime (risk-on/off rotation within crypto)
archetypes.mechanism: Crypto capital rotates along a risk curve anchored by Bitcoin. In early-bull and risk-off phases, money concentrates in BTC (the 'reserve asset' of crypto) and dominance rises; the 2024-2025 spot-ETF era amplified this because ETF flows are overwhelmingly BTC-only, structurally lifting dominance. In late-bull euphoria, having made multiples on BTC, capital chases higher-beta alts and dominance falls. The people 'on the other side' of a dominance call are traders fighting the prevailing regime — buying alts in a BTC-dominant tape (bleed vs BTC) or hiding in BTC during a genuine alt-season (underperformance). The signal is the *regime*, not the level of any single coin.
archetypes.typical_magnitude_pct: BTC.D swings of 15-35 percentage points across a full cycle; alt baskets can out/under-perform BTC by 50-200%+ over a rotation
archetypes.typical_lag: regime-scale (weeks-to-months); not an intraday signal
archetypes.typical_duration: months per regime leg
archetypes.recurrence: every cycle: BTC-led accumulation -> mid-cycle ETH/large-cap rotation -> late-cycle alt/meme blow-off -> risk-off flight back to BTC
leading_signals: BTC.D level and 30-day slope
Altcoin Season Index (>75 alt-season / <25 BTC season)
ETH/BTC ratio and its 50/200-day MA crosses
TOTAL2 / TOTAL ratio (alt share of market cap)
BTC spot-ETF net flows vs alt-ETF/alt-fund flows
days since last confirmed alt-season

historical_instances.trigger: 2021 alt-season: BTC dominance collapses as DOGE/BNB/altcoins blow off.
historical_instances.description: Through early 2021 BTC dominance fell from ~73% (January) to ~40% (mid-May) as capital rotated into BNB, DOGE, and the broad altcoin complex — a textbook late-cycle alt-season. The dominance bottom and alt euphoria immediately preceded the May 19 2021 leverage-driven crash.
historical_instances.volume_or_flow_change: Alt volumes overtook BTC; alt-season euphoria peaked just before the May-19 crash.

historical_instances.trigger: ETF-era BTC-led leg: BTC sets records ($122,838 on Jul 14 2025; $126,210 ATH Oct 6 2025) while dominance stays elevated.
historical_instances.description: The 2024-2025 cycle was unusually BTC-dominant: spot-ETF flows (BTC-only at first) concentrated demand in Bitcoin, which printed $122,838 on 2025-07-14 and an all-time high of $126,210 on 2025-10-06 while altcoins broadly lagged — the opposite of a classic alt-season. Dominance held in the high-50s/low-60s%, frustrating alt-season callers.
historical_instances.volume_or_flow_change: Spot-BTC-ETF inflows concentrated demand in BTC; alt share (TOTAL2) suppressed.

historical_instances.trigger: Bitcoin Season persists: BTC.D ~58-58.7%, Altcoin Season Index ~49, ~249 days since the last alt-season.
historical_instances.description: As of early June 2026 BTC dominance is ~58-58.7% with the Altcoin Season Index ~49 (below the 75 alt-season threshold) and ~249 days since the last confirmed alt-season — firmly 'Bitcoin Season.' A widely-circulated claim of a 'June 2026 alt-season rally' (with BTC at a $122,800 ATH and a $4.13T total market cap) is INCORRECT: those figures are a stale 2025 snapshot. In reality BTC was ~$63,500 and falling (-18% over the prior week) and total market cap was ~$2.35-2.5T — a risk-off drawdown, not an alt-season.
historical_instances.volume_or_flow_change: Risk-off drawdown; no alt-season rotation despite a minor 59.9%->58.7% dominance dip.

archetypes.name: Alt-season extreme as a late-cycle top signal
archetypes.mechanism: An alt-season is the last leg of the crypto risk curve: once BTC and ETH have run, the most speculative capital chases low-quality alts and memecoins. Historically the dominance *bottom* (alts most euphoric) coincides with cycle tops, because it marks maximum risk appetite and leverage with no higher-beta buyer left. The reversal — a sharp dominance spike as alts crater faster than BTC — is the risk-off 'flight to (crypto) quality' that confirms the regime has flipped. Traders fading the euphoria win; late alt-chasers are left holding the highest-beta bag into the drawdown.
archetypes.typical_magnitude_pct: post-peak alt baskets routinely draw down 70-90%+; BTC.D rebounds 15-30pp off the bottom
archetypes.typical_lag: the top signal is coincident-to-leading by days/weeks; confirmation on the dominance reversal
archetypes.typical_duration: the unwind runs the length of the ensuing bear (months-to-a-year)
archetypes.recurrence: once per cycle at the euphoria peak
leading_signals: Altcoin Season Index spiking >75
BTC.D making a multi-month low
memecoin/low-cap volume share at extremes (see [[memecoin-mania]])
ETH/BTC and alt/BTC ratios parabolic then rolling over
funding/leverage extremes across alts

historical_instances.trigger: ICO-era alt-mania drives BTC dominance to a cycle low (~33%) at the top.
historical_instances.description: At the January 2018 peak, ICO/altcoin mania drove BTC dominance down to ~33% as speculative capital flooded alts — the dominance bottom marked the cycle top, and the subsequent bear wiped 80-95% off most alts.
historical_instances.volume_or_flow_change: Alt volume share peaked; the 2018 bear followed.

historical_instances.trigger: 2021 alt-season bottom in dominance precedes the May-19 leverage crash.
historical_instances.description: The May 2021 dominance low (~40%) and peak alt euphoria immediately preceded the May-19 2021 leverage-driven crash (BTC -30%+ intraday). Classic alt-season-exhaustion -> risk-off reversal, with dominance rebounding as alts fell faster than BTC.
historical_instances.volume_or_flow_change: Flight to BTC quality as leverage unwound; see [[technical-signals]] and [[memecoin-mania]].

categories.summary: Every ~4 years (every 210,000 blocks) Bitcoin's mining reward is cut in half, halving the rate of new BTC supply hitting the market. Historically this has anchored a repeatable ~4-year cycle: a pre-halving rally into the event, a few months of post-halving chop/lag, then a 12-18 month bull run that tops out, followed by a deep bear market. The catch is diminishing returns — the halving-day-to-peak multiple has fallen from roughly 90x+ (2012) to ~30x (2016) to ~8x (2020) to only ~2x (2024) as Bitcoin's market cap has grown and ETF flows have changed price formation. CYCLE-BREAKER (4th halving): 2025 became the first-ever RED post-halving calendar year - BTC closed ~-6% (~$87,700, BELOW its January open), unprecedented vs the hugely-green 2012/2016/2020 post-halving years - with only ~+31% at the 1-year mark vs ~+300%/+567% in prior cycles. The supply-shock narrative is structurally fading: ETF flows and macro (S&P/Nasdaq) correlation now dominate the price signal far more than miner selling. The 4-year-cycle playbook may be over; the next halving (~2028) is already being floated as a 'buy the dip' narrative for 2026-27.

archetypes.name: Post-Halving Bull Cycle (the 12-18 month parabolic run)
archetypes.mechanism: The halving cuts new BTC issuance in half overnight (50->25->12.5->6.25->3.125 BTC/block). Daily new supply that miners must sell to cover costs drops sharply, while demand is steady or rising. With miners' structural sell-pressure halved, a smaller marginal buy-side flow is enough to clear the market at higher prices. The move is reflexive: rising price draws media attention and retail/institutional FOMO, which lifts price further, which draws more attention. Who is on the other side and loses: short sellers and skeptics who fade the rally early, and miners/early holders who sell into strength too soon. The top forms when the marginal buyer is exhausted and leverage is maxed; late entrants are left holding the bag into the subsequent bear market.
archetypes.typical_lag: weeks to months — the parabolic phase typically begins 3-6 months after the halving
archetypes.typical_duration: 12-18 months from halving to cycle top
archetypes.recurrence: once per ~4-year halving cycle; 4 occurrences 2012-2024
leading_signals: halving event occurs (block height multiple of 210,000)
stock-to-flow ratio doubles
miner reserves declining
BTC reclaims prior cycle ATH
rising spot ETF net inflows (2024+ cycle)
funding rates turning persistently positive

historical_instances.trigger: First Bitcoin halving at block 210,000; block reward cut 50 -> 25 BTC
historical_instances.description: Halving-day close ~$12.38. After a brief chop, BTC entered its first major parabolic run, reaching ~$1,100-$1,160 by late November 2013 (~12 months later), roughly a 90x+ gain.
historical_instances.volume_or_flow_change: New issuance halved from ~7,200 to ~3,600 BTC/day; trading volume expanded dramatically into the 2013 top

historical_instances.trigger: Second Bitcoin halving at block 420,000; block reward cut 25 -> 12.5 BTC
historical_instances.description: Halving-day price ~$640-$670. Sideways/choppy for months, then a strong run; roughly +290% to ~$2,500-$2,560 by the one-year anniversary (2017-07-09), peaking near $19,700-$19,800 on 2017-12-17 (~17 months), roughly a 30x gain from the halving day.
historical_instances.volume_or_flow_change: New issuance halved from ~3,600 to ~1,800 BTC/day; massive 2017 ICO-era volume expansion

historical_instances.trigger: Third Bitcoin halving at block 630,000; block reward cut 12.5 -> 6.25 BTC
historical_instances.description: Halving-day price ~$8,600 (one source: $8,601.80). Range-bound to upward initially, then a strong run peaking at ~$68,800 (often rounded to ~$69,000) on 2021-11-10 (~18 months), roughly a 7.5-8x gain. Double-top with an interim high near $64,800 in April 2021.
historical_instances.volume_or_flow_change: New issuance halved from ~1,800 to ~900 BTC/day; 2021 DeFi/NFT volume boom and institutional adoption (MicroStrategy, Tesla)

historical_instances.trigger: Fourth Bitcoin halving at block 840,000; block reward cut 6.25 -> 3.125 BTC
historical_instances.description: Halving-day price ~$64,000-$65,000 (~$64,994). Notably, BTC had already made a new ATH (~$73,750 on 2024-03-13) BEFORE the halving — a first, driven by spot-ETF demand. The post-halving run was the weakest on record: BTC topped at ~$126,223 on 2025-10-06, roughly a 2x gain from the halving day (vs 8x prior cycle), confirming diminishing returns. CYCLE-BREAKER: the halving fired Apr 19-20 2024 with BTC ~$63,762; the cycle then delivered only ~+31% at the 1-year mark (vs ~+300%/+567% in prior cycles) and - decisively - 2025 became the FIRST-EVER red post-halving calendar year (BTC ~-6% to ~$87,700, below its Jan open). BTC's correlation with the S&P 500/Nasdaq rose sharply (it traded as a macro/rates asset), and ETF flows displaced miner-selling as the dominant supply-side signal - structurally weakening the halving supply-shock thesis and the 4-year cycle itself.
historical_instances.volume_or_flow_change: New issuance halved from ~900 to ~450 BTC/day; US spot Bitcoin ETFs (launched Jan 2024) absorbed large sell pressure, changing price formation

archetypes.name: Pre-Halving Rally / Buy-the-Rumor
archetypes.mechanism: In the 6-12 months ahead of a halving, the upcoming supply cut is a well-telegraphed, calendar-certain event. Traders and investors front-run the expected post-halving scarcity narrative, accumulating into the event ('buy the rumor'). Demand front-loads while issuance is still at the old, higher rate, lifting price into the halving. Who is on the other side: late sellers and those who wait for the halving day itself to buy, who often find the easy gains already priced. In the 2024 cycle this front-running was so strong (amplified by ETF launch) that BTC set a new ATH BEFORE the halving for the first time ever — a structural shift in the pattern.
archetypes.typical_lag: builds over 6-12 months before the halving date
archetypes.typical_duration: 6-12 months pre-event
archetypes.typical_magnitude_pct: roughly +100% to +180% in the ~12 months leading into the halving
archetypes.recurrence: once per ~4-year cycle, ahead of each halving
leading_signals: halving date within 12 months
rising Google search interest in 'bitcoin halving'
accelerating spot ETF inflows (2024+)
BTC trending above 200-day moving average
improving funding/open-interest

historical_instances.trigger: Accumulation ahead of the May 2020 halving (buy-the-rumor)
historical_instances.description: Pre-halving rally ran roughly 10 months; BTC rose from ~$4,000 (mid-2019) toward ~$8,600 by the halving date despite the March 2020 COVID crash to ~$5,000 mid-window. Magnitude is an approximation from monthly price context.

archetypes.name: Post-Cycle Bear Market (the deflationary back half of the 4-year cycle)
archetypes.mechanism: After the post-halving bull tops out (~12-18 months post-halving), the reflexive process runs in reverse. Late buyers who entered on leverage near the top are underwater; as price falls, forced liquidations and capitulation cascade, and the same FOMO that drove the run becomes fear. Long-term holders who accumulated cheap distribute into the prior strength, becoming the supply overhang. The bear market typically erases 70-85% of the peak before the next pre-halving rally rebuilds a base. Who loses: top-buyers and over-levered longs; who wins: patient accumulators buying the capitulation low. This archetype is the bearish mirror image that completes the ~4-year cycle.
archetypes.typical_lag: begins immediately after the cycle top (~18mo post-halving)
archetypes.typical_duration: ~12 months of decline into the cycle low, roughly the second half of the 4-year cycle
archetypes.typical_magnitude_pct: -70% to -85% peak-to-trough
archetypes.recurrence: once per ~4-year cycle, following each bull-market top
leading_signals: BTC loses 200-day moving average after a parabolic run
funding rates flip persistently negative
exchange net-inflows spike (holders moving to sell)
failure to make new highs for >8 weeks after a blowoff top
long-term-holder distribution rising

historical_instances.volume_or_flow_change: Mt. Gox insolvency (~850k BTC) created a major supply/sentiment shock

historical_instances.trigger: Top of the 2024-halving cycle (~$126,223 on 2025-10-06) gives way to a sharp drawdown
historical_instances.description: After topping at ~$126,223 on 2025-10-06, BTC fell hard. CoinGecko measurement over 2025-06-04 to 2026-06-04 shows a max drawdown of -49.6% within the window, with price falling to a ~$62,854 low and ending ~$64,022 on 2026-06-04 (the period high in this window was $124,773 on the way up). The full peak-to-trough off the $126k ATH is roughly -50%. THE FULL 2025-26 BEAR EPISODE: total crypto market cap fell ~$4.4T (Oct 6 2025) to ~$2.4T (Q1 2026, ~-$2T); ETH -61.9% peak-to-trough ($4,926->$1,820), SOL ~-71%. The macro-trigger stack: the Oct-10-2025 record ~$19B liquidation cascade (Trump 100% China tariff) kicked it off; then Kevin Warsh's hawkish Fed-Chair nomination (Jan 30 2026, BTC -14% in 10d), broad tariff threats, $1B+ of BTC-ETF outflows, USDT-depeg FEARS (Feb 2026, no actual depeg), a Feb-5 Nasdaq-correlated drop, and MSTR's first BTC sale since 2022 (Jun 2026). A textbook diminishing-returns 4th-cycle: the shallowest post-halving top and a macro-driven, ETF-era bear correlated with risk assets rather than a pure crypto-credit blowup.
historical_instances.volume_or_flow_change: ETF outflows and deleveraging into the drawdown; bear unfolding as expected ~18-24 months post-halving

categories.summary: Public companies that put crypto (mostly BTC, later ETH/SOL) on their balance sheet move prices through three repeatable patterns. First, the initial "we're becoming a treasury company" announcement reliably rockets the company's own STOCK (Semler ~+30-37%, Metaplanet thousands of %, SharpLink roughly +1,800% to +2,600%) while only mildly nudging the coin. Second, sustained accumulation funded by issuing stock/debt at a premium to net asset value (mNAV) acts as a steady demand pump that absorbs supply during bull phases. Third — the dangerous mirror image — when the coin falls and the mNAV premium collapses toward or below 1.0, these leveraged holders face distribution and "death-spiral" forced-selling risk that adds reflexive sell-pressure (Tesla's 2022 $936M dump; the Nov 2025 BTC ~-15% slide amid treasury-firm stress).

archetypes.name: Treasury-Adoption Announcement (company pivots to crypto reserve)
archetypes.mechanism: A public company announces it is adopting BTC/ETH as a primary treasury reserve asset, usually copying the MicroStrategy playbook. The other side of the trade is equity investors who suddenly re-rate the stock as a leveraged, tax-advantaged crypto proxy (and short-sellers who get squeezed). The stock pops violently because the announcement converts a sleepy small-cap into a high-beta crypto vehicle that can trade at a large premium to the crypto it holds (mNAV > 1). The underlying coin moves much less — the announced first purchase is small relative to coin market cap and is often already bought before disclosure — so the coin reaction is mild-bullish to neutral, driven by sentiment/adoption narrative rather than flow.
affected_scope: other treasury-stock peers (MSTR, SMLR, MARA, SBET)
archetypes.typical_lag: immediate (same session, often pre-market)
archetypes.typical_duration: stock pop intraday-to-days; narrative read-across can persist weeks; coin effect <24h
archetypes.typical_magnitude_pct: +20% to +4000%+ on the announcing stock (size-dependent); BTC/ETH typically -2% to +3% same day
archetypes.recurrence: very frequent in 2024-2025 — dozens of copycats; cluster events around each new high-profile entrant
leading_signals: 8-K / press release announcing treasury policy adoption
board approval of crypto-as-reserve
convertible-note or ATM equity raise filed days before
small beaten-down small-cap with a crypto-aligned chairman
sudden short-interest spike then squeeze

historical_instances.trigger: Bitmine Immersion (BMNR) reaches 5,416,901 ETH (~4.49% of supply) - the largest corporate ETH treasury (MSTR of ETH).
historical_instances.description: Bitmine Immersion Technologies (BMNR, Tom Lee-linked) accumulated to 5,416,901 ETH (~4.49% of the ~120.7M supply, ~$10.9B at ~$2,003) by May 31 2026, plus 203 BTC and equity stakes - ~$11.6B total - pursuing an 'Alchemy of 5%' ETH-supply goal. It grew from ~4.1M ETH (Jan) to 5.4M (May), accumulating aggressively even as ETH fell ~40% YTD. The ETH analog of MicroStrategy's BTC flywheel: a structural demand sink, no clean single-day catalyst, with circular reflexivity/leverage risk if ETH keeps falling.

price_impacts.pct_change: structural demand (no clean single-day move; ETH fell ~40% YTD regardless)

historical_instances.volume_or_flow_change: 5.42M ETH (~4.49% of supply) accumulated; largest single corporate ETH holder.

historical_instances.trigger: SpaceX S-1 IPO filing discloses 18,712 BTC (fair value $1.293B).
historical_instances.description: SpaceX's S-1 (filed 2026-05-20, ahead of a record ~$1.75T IPO under ticker SPCX; roadshow ~Jun 4, trading ~Jun 12 2026) disclosed 18,712 BTC at fair value $1.293B / cost basis ~$661M (avg ~$35,300) as of Mar 31 2026 — more than double prior Arkham estimates. Notably SpaceX originally bought 25,724 BTC in 2021 and quietly sold ~27% down to 18,712 (corporate holders are not all diamond-hands). Ranks ~7th-8th among corporate holders (varies by source/methodology), ahead of Tesla (~11,509), Coinbase, and Block (~9,000), far behind Strategy's 843,706. The S-1 also disclosed a binding ~$45B compute agreement with Anthropic ($1.25B/mo through May 2029) for COLOSSUS/COLOSSUS II (post-xAI acquisition). A private-company disclosure of LEGACY holdings (no new buying) — no isolable BTC impact; BTC -2.3% over the window was the broad May-2026 downtrend. Tesla-SpaceX merger talks (May 2026) would create a combined ~30,221 BTC #5 holder.

price_impacts.window: May 18-22 2026 (broad downtrend; no isolable disclosure impact)

historical_instances.volume_or_flow_change: Disclosure only (no purchase); reveals a previously-private holder + a past ~7,000 BTC sell-down.

historical_instances.trigger: SharpLink Gaming (SBET) announces pivot to an ETH treasury vehicle (Joe Lubin chairman)

SBET spiked enormously from its base (sources range from ~+1,800% per Bybit, climbing ~$6.70 to ~$124, to ~+2,600% per ts2.tech) before later giving back ~86-92% from peak. Kicked off a wave of ETH treasury vehicles (later BitMine) that helped drive ETH +53% over late-June to mid-July 2025. Note: the ETH +53.3% window is a cohort-flywheel effect, not directly attributable to the SBET announcement day.

historical_instances.trigger: Semler Scientific (med-tech) announces BTC as primary treasury reserve, buys 581 BTC for ~$40M
historical_instances.description: Classic small-cap re-rate. A beaten-down ~$210M med-tech (down >30% YTD) became a BTC proxy overnight; stock soared while BTC barely moved — textbook stock-pops-coin-flat signature. Perplexity puts the day's close gain at ~30%; the +37% figure appears to reflect an intraday peak. BTC roughly flat to slightly down.

historical_instances.trigger: Metaplanet (Japan) announces it is adopting BTC as a treasury reserve asset; begins accumulation
historical_instances.description: Japanese 'MicroStrategy' birth (date 2024-04-08 confirmed). Stock ran thousands of percent over the following year on individual buy announcements; sources put the ~1-year gain anywhere from ~+4,000% to ~+8,850% depending on the endpoint chosen, so treat the precise multiple as approximate. The most extreme example of stock-side re-rating; coin effect negligible per-event.

historical_instances.trigger: Tesla discloses $1.5B BTC purchase (bought in January at ~$34,700 avg) and plans to accept BTC as payment
historical_instances.description: Largest, highest-profile corporate adoption to date. Unlike most adoptions, Tesla's size + Musk halo moved the COIN hard, not just the stock — BTC jumped ~14.5% same day (roughly from the low-$39,000s to over $44,000), hitting a new ATH. historical_instances.trigger: MicroStrategy announces first BTC purchase, 21,454 BTC for $250M at ~$11,653, calling BTC 'superior to cash' — the template for the entire category
historical_instances.description: First major US public-company treasury adoption. MSTR re-rated as a BTC proxy; launched the playbook every later entrant copied. BTC reaction muted; the durable effect was the template itself. Perplexity-confirmed: MSTR rose ~9% (from ~$123.62 to ~$134.89); BTC roughly flat same day.

archetypes.name: Sustained Accumulation / mNAV-Premium Flywheel (ongoing buys funded at a premium)
archetypes.mechanism: Once established, a treasury company runs a reflexive flywheel: because its stock trades at a premium to the crypto it holds (mNAV > 1), it issues equity/convertibles/preferreds ABOVE net asset value and uses the cash to buy more coin. Each buy is accretive (raises crypto-per-share), validating the premium, which lets it raise more — net new dry powder flowing INTO the coin. The other side is dilutive buyers of the stock who accept a premium for leveraged exposure, and coin sellers who get a persistent structural bid. Per individual buy the coin barely moves (each purchase is small vs daily volume), but in aggregate the cohort is a large, price-insensitive, sustained buyer. DATCos poured an estimated ~$42.7B into crypto in 2025 ($22.6B in Q3 alone per cited estimates), a real demand sink during the bull phase.
archetypes.typical_lag: diffuse — builds over weeks/quarters rather than per-event
archetypes.typical_duration: persists as long as mNAV > ~1 and capital markets stay open (multi-quarter)
archetypes.typical_magnitude_pct: individually negligible (<1-2% per buy); cohort contributes meaningfully to multi-month uptrends
archetypes.recurrence: continuous through 2024-2025 bull phase; weekly purchase 8-Ks from MSTR and peers
leading_signals: mNAV premium > 1 (especially > 2)
fresh ATM/convertible/preferred raise filings
weekly Monday MSTR purchase 8-Ks
rising 'BTC Yield'/coin-per-share metric
growing count of new treasury entrants (crowding)
exchange outflows to corporate custody addresses

historical_instances.trigger: Strategy (MSTR) reaches 843,706 BTC — ~3/4 of all corporate-held BTC; nearing its 1M target.
historical_instances.description: Strategy (formerly MicroStrategy) holds 843,706 BTC as of Jun 1 2026 (avg cost $66,385; ~$33.1B), ~3/4 of all corporate-treasury BTC and the largest corporate holder by far (e.g. >40x the largest private-company stacks). Late-April 2026 it added 3,273 BTC (~$255M) at 818,334. Ongoing accumulation — a structural demand sink, not a single-day catalyst.
historical_instances.volume_or_flow_change: 843,706 BTC held; persistent ATM-funded accumulation toward a 1M-BTC target.

historical_instances.trigger: Q3 2025: Digital Asset Treasury Companies (DATCos) estimated to deploy ~$22.6B into crypto in a single quarter (of ~$42.7B for full-year 2025, per cited estimates)
historical_instances.description: Quantifies the flywheel at cohort scale. This wall of premium-funded buying supported BTC's climb to the >$126k October peak before the premium unwind began. The dollar-deployment figures are third-party estimates, not audited totals, hence medium confidence; the BTC trend move is approximate.

price_impacts.pct_change: to >$126k peak (~+100% from early-2025 lows; approximate)

historical_instances.trigger: ETH-treasury accumulation wave: SharpLink + new entrants (BitMine, Tom Lee) ramp ETH buying through summer 2025
historical_instances.description: Cohort flywheel applied to ETH. Aggregate treasury demand coincided with ETH +53% over late-June to mid-July 2025 (CoinGecko-measured); BitMine ultimately accumulated ~3-5% of all ETH supply, a major structural demand sink.

historical_instances.trigger: MicroStrategy buys ~27,200 BTC for ~$2.03B (Oct 31-Nov 10) at ~$74,463 avg; part of post-election mega-accumulation
historical_instances.description: Peak-flywheel example: equity-funded multi-billion buys during BTC's run to $100k+, with mNAV premium reported near ~4x in late 2024. The cohort's relentless premium-funded bid coincided with the strongest leg of the 2024 bull market. The BTC and MSTR moves below are multi-week trend figures, not attributable to this single 8-K.

historical_instances.trigger: Marathon Digital (MARA) adopts full 'HODL' policy — retains all mined BTC and buys on open market (e.g. ~4,144 BTC / ~$249M in Aug 2024 at ~$59,500)
historical_instances.description: Miner-turned-accumulator variant: converts a flow seller (miners normally sell coin to cover costs) into a holder/buyer, removing sell-pressure (supply-restriction) and adding demand. Stock becomes a leveraged BTC play. Price impacts are qualitative/structural rather than a measured single-event move; left as estimates.

archetypes.name: Distribution / Forced Selling / mNAV-Discount Unwind (the flywheel in reverse)
archetypes.mechanism: The reflexive flywheel runs backward. When the coin falls and the stock's premium collapses to or below net asset value (mNAV <= 1), accretive equity issuance stops, and pressure builds to monetize: either an outright corporate sale (Tesla 2022) or, for over-leveraged firms with debt covenants, forced liquidation to defend mNAV / service debt. Selling pushes the coin down, which drops more firms' mNAV below 1, forcing more sales — a 'death spiral.' The other side is whoever absorbs the supply at distressed prices. Direct corporate sales tend to produce a modest, sentiment-driven coin dip (the sale is often already done), but the SYSTEMIC risk — a cohort holding a large share of supply being forced out together — is the real bearish tail, amplified by leveraged longs in the treasury stocks unwinding simultaneously.
affected_scope: treasury-stock cohort (MSTR, MARA, SBET, BMNR, Metaplanet)
archetypes.typical_lag: sentiment dip immediate; systemic forced-selling plays out over days-to-weeks
archetypes.typical_duration: single-sale dip hours-to-days; mNAV-unwind regimes persist weeks-to-months
archetypes.typical_magnitude_pct: -2% to -5% coin on a single disclosed corporate sale; -10% to -25% coin during a full premium-collapse/forced-selling regime
archetypes.recurrence: rare for outright sales (Strategy held through all of 2023-2025); systemic discount-unwind episodes clustered in late 2025
leading_signals: mNAV falling toward / below 1.0
CEO comments about 'conditions to sell'
treasury stock down >50% YoY while coin down less (premium gone)
debt/preferred coverage stress, covenant headlines
treasury-stock share-buyback announcements (cash drain)
'one in three treasuries below NAV' breadth
rising correlation of treasury stocks crashing together

historical_instances.trigger: Strategy (MSTR) discloses its first BTC SALE since 2022 - 32 BTC (~$2.5M) for a preferred-dividend payment.
historical_instances.description: Strategy's 8-K (Jun 1 2026) disclosed selling just 32 BTC (~$2.5M at ~$77,135) between May 26-31 to fund preferred-stock distributions - the first confirmed sale since Dec 2022 and a microscopic 0.0038% of its 843,706-BTC stack. Materially trivial, but the 'Saylor never-sell era cracks' NARRATIVE rattled the market: BTC fell ~4.5% to ~$69,800 (Jun 1-2; part of the broader June-2026 risk-off to ~$63k) amid $2.43B of May spot-BTC-ETF outflows (largest of 2026). A textbook case of a symbolically-loaded but fundamentally-immaterial event moving price via narrative, not flow - the inverse of the accumulation flywheel's reflexivity.

price_impacts.window: Jun 1-2 2026 (8-K disclosure, to ~$69,800; broader June crash to ~$63k)

historical_instances.volume_or_flow_change: 32 BTC sold (~$2.5M, 0.0038% of holdings) for a dividend; $2.43B May BTC-ETF outflows (largest of 2026).

historical_instances.trigger: Trump Media transfers 2,650 BTC (~$205M) to Crypto.com (says 'did not sell').
historical_instances.description: Trump Media & Technology Group moves 2,650 BTC (~$205M) to Crypto.com, its 2nd large exchange transfer, amid steep losses (bought 11,542 BTC at avg $118,522, down ~$455M; Q1 net loss $405.9M; withdrew its spot-BTC-ETF application). Feared as distribution, but no isolable impact — BTC +0.44% over the window. Illustrates that exchange transfers != confirmed sells and that small-treasury distress is noise vs market.
historical_instances.volume_or_flow_change: 2,650 BTC to a CEX (custody/liquidity/possible-sale ambiguous; company says not sold).

historical_instances.trigger: SharpLink (SBET) ETH-treasury vehicle down ~86-92% from peak; ETH-treasury cohort de-rates as premiums vanish
historical_instances.description: Shows the unwind extends to the ETH cohort. The same vehicles that amplified ETH's summer rally became forced/willing distributors and de-rating stocks as mNAV collapsed, with BitMine sitting on multi-billion paper losses. SBET drawdown from peak is ~86% (ts2.tech) to ~92.5% (Bybit); the BitMine ~$8.66B paper-loss figure is a single forward-dated (2026) source, so this instance stays medium/low confidence.

historical_instances.trigger: Strategy CEO Phong Le comments on conditions under which the firm would sell BTC; reports note MSTR market cap falling near/below its BTC holdings, with MSTR down sharply YoY (~-45% to -63% per varying sources)
historical_instances.description: Sentiment-shock on the mere prospect of the largest holder selling. BTC traded toward/below $86k in early December; demonstrates how reflexive-deleveraging fear in the treasury cohort transmits to spot even without an actual sale (Strategy did not sell BTC in 2025). The precise '-60% YoY' is endpoint-dependent — Perplexity cites figures from ~-45% (from Nov peak) to ~-63% (1-year), so a range is used and confidence stays medium. The 'below $86k toward ~$82k' figure is approximate/web-sourced.

historical_instances.trigger: Strategy (MSTR) mNAV (market cap to BTC NAV) falls below 1.0 for the first time as BTC slides from ~$107k toward $95k; reports cite roughly one-in-three treasury firms dropping below NAV
historical_instances.description: First time the category leader's NAV multiple slipped below 1.0 (corroborated by Cointelegraph/TradingView coverage around 2025-11-10/11-12; it recovered above ~1.09 shortly after). The earlier '0.97x' precision was unsourced and is removed; the exact sub-1 low is not published. This removed accretive issuance and crystallized 'death-spiral' fears — the regime flip from flywheel to unwind, with treasury stocks selling off as a correlated cohort. BTC move is CoinGecko-measured.

historical_instances.trigger: BTC tops ~$126k (early Oct) then rolls over; premium-funded buying stalls as mNAVs compress across the cohort
historical_instances.description: The top of the 2025 flywheel and start of the unwind. As the premium that fueled accumulation evaporated, the marginal treasury bid disappeared, removing structural support. BTC move CoinGecko-measured for the Oct 5-15 window.

historical_instances.trigger: Tesla reveals it converted ~75% of its BTC to fiat in Q2 2022 (~$936M, ~30k BTC sold at ~$29k avg), citing China-COVID cash needs
historical_instances.description: The canonical corporate distribution event. Reaction was modest because the sale was already executed during the quarter and was framed as idiosyncratic (cash management, not a BTC view) — BTC dipped then recovered. Perplexity confirms the direction (BTC fell) but did not corroborate an exact same-day percentage from one source; the widely reported ~-2% intraday figure is retained as approximate. Confirms single disclosed sales are weak coin catalysts vs the announcement-buy asymmetry.

categories.summary: DeFi narratives move prices through a repeatable life-cycle: a new yield mechanism (liquidity mining, restaking points, RWA tokenization) ignites a reflexive TVL land-grab that pumps governance tokens hundreds to thousands of percent, capital rotates into the sector, then the eventual token-generation/airdrop event becomes a "sell the news" distribution as farmers dump. Layered on top are vote-bribe "liquidity wars" that bid up emission-control tokens, and periodic reflexive deleveraging events (exploits, founder-collateral liquidations, yield unwinds) that crater the whole sector at once. Net: violent up-moves on narrative ignition, chronic underperformance of governance tokens versus the underlying TVL growth, and sharp correlated drawdowns when leverage unwinds.

archetypes.name: Narrative ignition / TVL land-grab (yield-farming, restaking points, RWA hype)
archetypes.mechanism: A novel yield mechanism (liquidity mining 2020, restaking points 2023-24, tokenized treasuries 2024) creates a reflexive loop: emissions/points subsidize deposits, TVL spikes, the governance token pumps on the story, higher token price funds richer emissions, attracting more TVL. The other side is late retail and rotators buying the narrative from early farmers/VCs who accumulated cheap and harvest the emissions. Early movers win; the eventual marginal buyer holds the bag once emissions normalize.
leading_signals: sharp TVL inflow before token price moves
launch of a points/airdrop program
first major institutional validation (e.g. BlackRock BUIDL)
rising social/search interest in the meta
copycat protocols launching same week
archetypes.typical_lag: immediate to days for the flagship token; days-to-weeks for read-across into peer tokens
archetypes.typical_duration: weeks to a few months for the acute pump; the narrative regime can persist 1-2 quarters
archetypes.typical_magnitude_pct: +100% to +1,000%+ on flagship tokens during ignition; outlier fair-launch tokens (YFI) >100,000%
archetypes.recurrence: roughly one major sector-defining wave every 12-24 months (2020 DeFi Summer, 2021 Curve/L1, 2023-24 restaking, 2024 RWA)

historical_instances.trigger: Tokenized RWAs (ex-stablecoins) surge to ~$25-32B in 2026; Ethereum + L2s dominate.
historical_instances.description: Tokenized real-world assets (ex-stablecoins) grew ~4x YoY to ~$26.4B by Mar 2026 (~$31-32B by May), with Ethereum hosting >60% and settlement increasingly on L2s (Base, Arbitrum). Composition: tokenized US Treasuries are the largest class (~$10B in Feb -> $13.4B by Apr, per RWA.xyz), then tokenized commodities/gold (~$5.9-7.3B) and private credit (~$4B+). BlackRock's BUIDL (~$2.24-2.4B across ~5 chains) and JPMorgan's Kinexys lead institutionally. KEY ROTATION DYNAMIC: in the 2026 risk-off tape, capital rotated FROM higher-risk DeFi yield-farming INTO lower-risk tokenized Treasuries as TVL dipped - on-chain 'flight to safety' (tokenized T-bills as the crypto-native cash leg). A structural fundamental tailwind, NOT a clean spot catalyst - every RWA milestone in 2026 coincided with crypto falling (ETH -40% YTD). Forward context (not price events): industry forecasts ~$16T tokenized by 2030; the BIS/IIF Project Agora (7 central banks, 40+ institutions) proved atomic wholesale cross-border settlement - but it is PERMISSIONED central-bank/bank infra, not a public-crypto-token catalyst.

price_impacts.pct_change: structural fundamental tailwind (no clean spot catalyst; ETH -40% YTD regardless)

historical_instances.volume_or_flow_change: RWA TVL ~$25-32B in 2026 (Ethereum >60%); BlackRock BUIDL multi-chain, JPMorgan Kinexys.

historical_instances.trigger: Aave (Horizon market) becomes the first DeFi lending protocol to cross $1B in tokenized-RWA deposits.
historical_instances.description: Aave's Horizon market crossed $1B in real-world-asset deposits on Feb 19 2026 (up from ~$600M in January) — the first lending protocol to do so, concentrated in tokenized bonds/treasury-like products attracting institutions. Aave DAO revenue hit $142M in 2025 (> the prior three years combined). A structural institutional-DeFi-adoption milestone, supportive of AAVE fundamentals but not a clean spot catalyst (it coincided with broad crypto outflows). Part of the wider RWA ramp (total tokenized RWA ~$25-32B in 2026).

price_impacts.pct_change: fundamental milestone (no clean spot catalyst; coincided with broad outflows)

historical_instances.volume_or_flow_change: RWA deposits $600M (Jan) -> $1B (Feb 19 2026); tokenized bonds/treasuries led.

historical_instances.description: BlackRock's BUIDL fund launched on 2024-03-20, institutionally validating tokenized real-world assets. ONDO (RWA flagship) rode the narrative from its ~Jan 2024 launch to a ~$2.14 ATH on 2024-12-16, roughly +1,200% launch-to-peak.
historical_instances.volume_or_flow_change: Tokenized RWA market (ex-stablecoins) reached ~$15B+ by late 2024
