historical_instances.description: EigenLayer's staged mainnet restaking launch began in June 2023 (exact day not confirmed across sources; full Stage-2 rollout came Apr 2024), launching the 'points' meta. LRT protocols (ether.fi, Renzo, Kelp) layered their own points on top, driving billions in TVL and a year-long restaking narrative ahead of token launches.
historical_instances.volume_or_flow_change: EigenLayer TVL grew to ~$15B+ at peak; LRT TVL into the billions; Pendle TVL expanded on points/yield trading

historical_instances.trigger: SushiSwap vampire attack on Uniswap — SUSHI emissions bribe LPs to migrate liquidity
historical_instances.description: SushiSwap used SUSHI token emissions to drain Uniswap liquidity, demonstrating the emissions-as-acquisition mechanic; SUSHI spiked then faded after migration. Exact launch-week percentage is an estimate.
historical_instances.volume_or_flow_change: ~$1B+ liquidity temporarily migrated from Uniswap to SushiSwap

historical_instances.trigger: yearn.finance YFI fair launch (no premine, pure liquidity-mining distribution)
historical_instances.description: YFI launched ~2020-07-17 at roughly $30-32 and rose to approximately $43,000 by September 2020 (~+143,000%) per CoinGecko's summary — the archetypal fair-launch yield-farming moonshot.
historical_instances.volume_or_flow_change: Yearn TVL surged on emissions; spawned vault-aggregator subsector

historical_instances.trigger: Compound launches COMP liquidity-mining distribution, kicking off DeFi Summer
historical_instances.description: COMP governance-token distribution began on 2020-06-16. COMP traded ~$64-67 at launch and surged to roughly $372 by the end of the first day (market cap from ~$30M toward ~$2B on circulating-supply estimates), overtaking MKR's market cap almost immediately and igniting the yield-farming meta. Exact intraday figures vary by source.
historical_instances.volume_or_flow_change: COMP market cap reached the billions within ~24h, overtaking MKR; triggered sector-wide TVL surge

archetypes.name: Points/airdrop 'sell the news' (TGE distribution dump)
archetypes.mechanism: Farmers accumulate points/eligibility expecting an airdrop. At the token-generation event (TGE) / claim, recipients who farmed for free have ~100% cost basis as profit and dump immediately into thin launch liquidity. VCs/team unlocks compound supply. The other side is narrative buyers and exchange listing FOMO who absorb the sell pressure. Recipients win, late spot buyers lose. Exceptions occur when float is tiny and the protocol has genuine product-market fit (HYPE), which can invert the dump into a squeeze.
leading_signals: TGE/claim date announcement
large airdrop allocation %
high FDV vs low initial float
pre-market/IOU price far above implied float value
negative perp funding pre-listing
Dune dashboards showing rapid claim+sell
archetypes.typical_lag: immediate (hours to first 7 days post-claim)
archetypes.typical_duration: the acute dump is days; the bleed often continues weeks-to-months as cliffs/emissions add supply
archetypes.typical_magnitude_pct: -30% to -85% from listing/ATH within weeks for typical farm-and-dump tokens
archetypes.recurrence: every major TGE in a hot meta — dozens per cycle; highly repeatable

historical_instances.trigger: Uniswap UNI airdrop (400 UNI to every past user)
historical_instances.description: Dune analysis: >75% of recipient wallets sold within 7 days and ~80% within 30 days; most cashed out at ~$2-$4 — the canonical airdrop-dump dataset. (The catalog's '85% within 90 days' is from the same Dune report but was not separately re-confirmed here.)
historical_instances.volume_or_flow_change: Massive claim-and-sell volume; mostly exited at $2-$4

historical_instances.trigger: Arbitrum ARB airdrop / TGE
historical_instances.description: ARB launched/claimed 2023-03-23 amid chaotic IOU/futures pricing (early prints reported as high as ~$8.67) before settling. It fell quickly to ~$1.20, briefly bounced to ~$1.53 on Mar 24, and traded around $1.20-1.25 by 2023-03-27. The exact 'percent drop' depends heavily on which launch reference price is used, so a precise figure is not reliable.

price_impacts.pct_change: settled to ~$1.20-1.25 within days of a much higher launch print

historical_instances.volume_or_flow_change: Heavy launch-day sell volume across fragmented venues

historical_instances.trigger: Ethena ENA airdrop/claim opens; ATH 2024-04-11 then chronic bleed
historical_instances.description: ENA opened claims ~2024-04-02, peaked at ~$1.52 on 2024-04-11, then declined through 2024, bottoming at ~$0.1953 on 2024-09-06 — roughly an 87% drawdown from the ATH as airdrop supply and unlocks hit. (The catalog's '~$0.28 by mid-2024' was imprecise; the corroborated 2024 low was ~$0.195 in September.)
historical_instances.volume_or_flow_change: $750M ENA airdrop; high sell pressure post-claim

historical_instances.trigger: Post-TGE supply overhang + sector derating measured over trailing 12 months (ENA, PENDLE)
historical_instances.description: Trailing-year CoinGecko measurement of post-airdrop yield/restaking-meta tokens shows continued bleed and brutal drawdowns: ENA -66.5% (intra-window max DD -90.2%), PENDLE -68.3% (intra-window max DD -83.4%) — the chronic supply-overhang tail of the sell-the-news archetype.
historical_instances.volume_or_flow_change: Ongoing emission/unlock supply vs fading narrative demand

historical_instances.trigger: Hyperliquid HYPE airdrop — COUNTER-EXAMPLE (squeeze not dump)
historical_instances.description: HYPE launched ~2024-11-29 at roughly $2 and ripped higher, reaching ~$9.8 by Dec 1 2024 (~5x from launch) — inverting the dump pattern due to tiny float, no VC unlocks, and real product traction. Shows when the archetype fails.
historical_instances.volume_or_flow_change: Billion-dollar airdrop yet buy pressure dominated due to low float / no team-VC sell side

archetypes.name: Vote-bribe / liquidity wars (Curve wars, veTokenomics)
archetypes.mechanism: veTokenomics let token-lockers direct emissions to pools. Protocols needing deep liquidity bribe lockers to vote for their pool, creating demand for the emission-control token (CRV) and meta-governance layers (CVX). The other side: protocols paying bribes and late buyers of the governance token; lockers and the meta-governance layer capture the bribe yield. Bullish during the land-grab as bribe yields bid up the token; structurally decays as emissions inflate supply and bribe markets normalize.
leading_signals: rising bribe APRs
increasing lock ratios / falling liquid float
a new protocol entering the war (buying votes)
meta-governance TVL inflows (Convex)
archetypes.typical_lag: days-to-weeks during accumulation; the structural decay plays out over many months
archetypes.typical_duration: the war phase lasts months; token decay persists for years post-peak
archetypes.typical_magnitude_pct: several hundred to ~+2,000%+ on the meta-governance token during the war (range depends on launch reference price); then -90%+ structural decline
archetypes.recurrence: the canonical instance was 2021-22; the veTokenomics template recurs across protocols (Balancer, Velodrome, etc.) but rarely at the same scale

historical_instances.trigger: Convex Finance launches; battle to control CRV emissions (Curve wars) escalates
historical_instances.description: CVX launched mid-May 2021 (exact day not confirmed across sources) and reached an ATH around $60 on/near 2022-01-01 as Convex accumulated CRV vote power; Convex TVL surpassed $20B in Jan 2022 — the war's liquidity peak. The launch-to-peak gain is variously cited from ~+950% (off a ~$5.72 base) to ~+2,300% (off an early ~$2.47 print), so the precise multiple is uncertain.
historical_instances.volume_or_flow_change: Convex TVL >$20B around Jan 2022; large CRV lock-up

historical_instances.trigger: Curve wars peak then multi-year structural decay of emission tokens
historical_instances.description: After the Jan 2022 peak, CVX and CRV entered a prolonged decline as emissions inflated supply, bribe yields normalized, and the broader 2022 bear hit DeFi — the structural-decay leg of veTokenomics. The -90%+ figure is an estimate of the peak-to-2023-lows decline.
historical_instances.volume_or_flow_change: Emissions-driven supply inflation; declining bribe markets

archetypes.name: DeFi reflexive deleveraging / contagion (exploits, founder-collateral, yield unwind)
archetypes.mechanism: DeFi yields are often built on leverage and recursive collateral (looping, LST/LRT leverage, founder borrows against governance token). A shock — an exploit, a depeg, or a yield collapse — forces liquidations. Liquidations sell the token into thin liquidity, dropping price, triggering more liquidations (reflexive). Contagion spreads to correlated lending/governance tokens and to any large concentrated borrow position. The other side: liquidators and short sellers profit; leveraged farmers and lenders eat losses/bad debt.
leading_signals: a single large borrow position near liquidation (e.g. founder collateral)
stablecoin/LST depeg starting
exploit/hack headline
spiking lending utilization
TVL outflow acceleration
negative funding flush
archetypes.typical_lag: immediate (minutes to hours) for the levered token; hours-to-days for contagion read-across
archetypes.typical_duration: acute drop is hours-to-days; sentiment/TVL recovery can take weeks-to-months
archetypes.typical_magnitude_pct: -10% to -25% on the directly affected token in 24h; -5% to -15% sector contagion
archetypes.recurrence: multiple times per cycle — exploits and forced unwinds are frequent in DeFi

historical_instances.trigger: KelpDAO LRT-collateral exploit cascades into a $15B+ Aave TVL flush (DeFi-wide ~$13B in 2 days).
historical_instances.description: The $292M KelpDAO rsETH exploit (see hacks-exploits) cascaded through DeFi: Aave TVL fell from ~$48.5B to ~$30.7B by Apr 22 (~$15B+ in 4 days) and DeFi-wide TVL dropped ~$13B in two days as users de-risked from liquid-restaking-token (LRT) collateral. ~$196M Aave bad debt; AAVE -17.86%. Structural lesson: LRTs as recursive cross-protocol collateral amplify contagion (the DeFi analog of rehypothecation). Resolution: Aave governance temporarily adjusted the rsETH oracle to liquidate the hacker's positions cleanly on Ethereum + Arbitrum, recovering ~$20M and leaving ~10% short of full bad-debt recovery; the 'DeFi United' effort contributed >$320M. DeFi-wide TVL fell >7% in a single day on the breach.
historical_instances.volume_or_flow_change: Aave TVL ~$48.5B->~$30.7B; DeFi-wide ~$13B in 2 days.

historical_instances.trigger: Trailing-12-month measure of DeFi/yield-token deleveraging and sector derating (CoinGecko)
historical_instances.description: Over the trailing year DeFi governance/yield tokens posted deep drawdowns consistent with repeated leverage unwinds and fading narratives. CoinGecko intra-window max drawdowns: ENA -90.2%, PENDLE -83.4%, ONDO -80.3%, MKR -57.2%, SKY -56.7%, HYPE -64.3% — even though HYPE finished +105.4% net, showing how violent the deleveraging swings are.
historical_instances.volume_or_flow_change: Repeated leverage flushes; correlated sector drawdowns

historical_instances.trigger: Curve Vyper reentrancy exploit drains multiple pools; Egorov's ~$60-100M CRV-backed loans threaten bad debt
historical_instances.description: A Vyper reentrancy bug was exploited 2023-07-30, draining several Curve pools. CRV fell roughly 13% on the day (some coverage cites a larger intraday peak-to-trough, with prints near $0.58-0.48). Egorov's concentrated CRV-backed borrowing (~$60M on Aave V2, with total CRV-backed loans reported up to ~$100M) raised systemic liquidation fear across DeFi. Exploit-loss reporting ranges from ~$47M initial to >$70M total; broader at-risk TVL was withdrawn from affected pools.
historical_instances.volume_or_flow_change: ~$47M initial exploit loss, >$70M reported across the attacks; large at-risk TVL withdrawn from Curve pools; lending markets paused/scrutinized

categories.summary: When a major crypto exchange or lender becomes insolvent and freezes customer funds, the market reacts violently: prices of the affected platform's token collapse near-zero, and fear spreads to Bitcoin and the broadest market because users no longer trust anyone holding their coins. These collapses cluster — one failure (e.g. Terra/Luna) bankrupts the lenders exposed to it (3AC, Celsius, Voyager), whose failures then bankrupt the firms exposed to THEM (BlockFi, Genesis), producing a multi-month contagion cascade rather than a single drop. A distinct, slower pattern is the "estate overhang," where coins recovered from a defunct exchange (Mt Gox) are eventually returned to creditors who are expected to sell, weighing on price for months in anticipation.

archetypes.name: Acute Insolvency / Bank-Run Freeze
archetypes.mechanism: A platform reveals (or is revealed) to be insolvent and freezes withdrawals. Depositors who believed their balance was money they could withdraw on demand discover the platform was running a fractional-reserve / leveraged book against their assets. The platform's native token (which was often the thing propping up the balance sheet) collapses toward zero as the reflexive loop — token used as collateral, falling token destroys collateral value, forcing more selling — runs in reverse. Bitcoin and the broad market sell off because every holder re-prices counterparty risk simultaneously and rushes to self-custody or to exit entirely. The other side: leveraged longs and the platform's own token holders are forcibly wiped; the winners are short sellers and those who withdrew early or held self-custody.
affected_scope: ecosystem tokens tied to the failed entity
leading_signals: Leaked balance sheet showing native-token concentration (CoinDesk's FTX/Alameda report on 2022-11-02)
A large rival publicly announcing it will dump its holdings of the platform's token (CZ's 2022-11-06 tweet)
Anomalous exchange outflows / large BTC moving off the platform before the halt
Token trading at a discount on the affected venue vs. other exchanges
Withdrawal delays / 'temporary pause due to extreme market conditions' language
Negative funding and rising borrow rates on the token
archetypes.typical_lag: immediate to hours — the token gaps down within minutes of the trigger; BTC/broad-market reacts within hours to 1-2 days
archetypes.typical_duration: Token impact is permanent (near-total loss). Broad-market acute drop plays out over 3-10 days, with a depressed regime lasting weeks to months.
archetypes.typical_magnitude_pct: Platform token: -70% to -95% in 24-72h. BTC: -8% to -20% over the acute window. Closely-tied ecosystem coins: -40% to -60%.
archetypes.recurrence: Episodic but recurring across every cycle — roughly one major instance per bear-market liquidity crunch (2014 Gox, 2022 Celsius/FTX). Smaller exchange freezes occur more frequently.

historical_instances.trigger: CoinDesk report (Nov 2) exposed Alameda Research's balance sheet was dominated by FTX's own FTT token; Binance CEO CZ announced (Nov 6) he would liquidate ~$500M of FTT; a bank run on FTX followed and FTX halted withdrawals on Nov 8, with Binance signing a non-binding LOI to acquire it the same day (later abandoned).
historical_instances.description: FTX/Alameda insolvency. FTT collapsed and FTX froze customer funds; FTX, Alameda and 100+ entities filed Chapter 11 on Nov 11, 2022.
historical_instances.volume_or_flow_change: Massive BTC/stablecoin outflows from centralized exchanges to self-custody; spike in liquidations; FTT volume exploded then evaporated to near-zero post-halt.

historical_instances.trigger: Mt Gox — then the largest Bitcoin exchange — halted all BTC withdrawals on Feb 7, suspended trading Feb 24, and filed for bankruptcy protection in Tokyo on Feb 28, citing ~850,000 BTC missing/stolen.
historical_instances.description: Mt Gox insolvency, the original crypto exchange collapse. BTC quoted on Gox fell to a deep discount versus other exchanges during the freeze.

price_impacts.window: 1 Feb 2014 to end of March 2014 (Mt Gox problem period)

price_impacts.pct_change: deep discount (Gox-quoted price fell far below other venues during freeze)

historical_instances.volume_or_flow_change: Gox order book froze; arbitrage gap blew out as Gox BTC became unwithdrawable.

historical_instances.trigger: Celsius Network paused all withdrawals, swaps and transfers on June 12, 2022 citing 'extreme market conditions,' revealing it could not meet redemptions on its loan book.
historical_instances.description: Celsius bank-run freeze, the first domino of the post-Luna lender contagion (also feeds the contagion archetype). Celsius filed Chapter 11 on July 13, 2022.
historical_instances.volume_or_flow_change: stETH/ETH peg widened sharply as Celsius and 3AC were forced sellers; liquidation cascade across lending desks.

historical_instances.trigger: Genesis Global Capital halted withdrawals on its lending arm on Nov 16, 2022 due to FTX exposure; Gemini Earn (which routed customer funds to Genesis) froze ~$900M of customer redemptions the same day.
historical_instances.description: Genesis/Gemini Earn freeze — direct second-order FTX contagion. Genesis filed Chapter 11 on Jan 19, 2023.
historical_instances.volume_or_flow_change: GUSD traded at a discount; further exchange outflows.

archetypes.name: Contagion Cascade / Counterparty Domino
archetypes.mechanism: Crypto lenders and funds form a tightly interconnected credit web — the same collateral (often a token like LUNA, GBTC, or stETH) is rehypothecated across multiple desks. When one large borrower (Three Arrows Capital) defaults, every lender exposed to it (Voyager, Genesis, BlockFi, Celsius) takes a hole in its balance sheet, is forced to liquidate collateral into a falling market, and may itself become insolvent — bankrupting the next firm down the chain. This is reflexive forced-deleveraging: selling begets lower prices begets more margin calls begets more selling. Unlike the acute single-venue freeze, this archetype grinds the whole market lower over weeks-to-months as each domino falls. The other side: anyone who lent unsecured to these funds loses; macro/spot holders who deleveraged early survive; the structural lesson is that opaque rehypothecation concentrates tail risk.
leading_signals: A large fund missing a margin call / a public notice of default issued to it (Voyager's default notice to 3AC, Jun 2022)
A widening stETH or GBTC discount (forced sellers exiting locked collateral)
A 'systemically important' token already collapsing (LUNA in May 2022 preceded the lender failures)
Lenders cutting yields / pausing new deposits / seeking emergency lines (Celsius, BlockFi raising rescue capital)
Falling aggregate open interest as leverage is purged
Rumors of insolvency clustering across multiple named firms within days
archetypes.typical_lag: days to weeks — the first default is acute, but the full cascade unfolds over 1-6 months as each linked entity fails in sequence.
archetypes.typical_duration: Months. The 2022 cascade ran from the May Luna shock through the November FTX failure and into the January 2023 Genesis bankruptcy — roughly 8 months of grinding decline / suppressed prices.
archetypes.typical_magnitude_pct: BTC: -50% to -65% peak-to-trough over the full cascade. ETH and large alts: -70% to -80%. The compounding of multiple shocks, not any single one, drives the depth.
archetypes.recurrence: Once per major leverage build-up / bear cycle. The 2022 episode is the canonical full cascade; isolated defaults that don't chain (single fund blow-ups) are more frequent.

historical_instances.trigger: Three Arrows Capital (3AC), a multi-billion-dollar crypto hedge fund, defaulted after its LUNA/stETH/GBTC bets imploded; Voyager Digital issued a notice of default on a >$665M loan; a BVI court ordered 3AC into liquidation on June 27, 2022.
historical_instances.description: 3AC default — the keystone of the 2022 contagion. Its failure directly holed Voyager, Genesis, BlockFi and Celsius.
historical_instances.volume_or_flow_change: Forced unwinds of stETH and GBTC positions; lending desks margin-called across the board.

historical_instances.trigger: Voyager Digital suspended all trading, deposits and withdrawals on July 1, 2022, then filed for Chapter 11 bankruptcy on July 5, 2022 after 3AC defaulted on its $665M loan.
historical_instances.description: Voyager bankruptcy — second domino, direct 3AC contagion.
historical_instances.volume_or_flow_change: VGX collapsed; customer funds locked.

historical_instances.trigger: BlockFi filed for Chapter 11 bankruptcy, citing exposure to FTX/Alameda (FTX had extended BlockFi a credit line earlier in 2022, then itself collapsed).
historical_instances.description: BlockFi bankruptcy — third-order contagion (3AC hurt it in summer, FTX finished it in November).
historical_instances.volume_or_flow_change: Further confidence drain from CeFi yield products; continued exchange outflows.

historical_instances.trigger: Genesis Global Capital filed for Chapter 11 bankruptcy, the final major domino, after halting withdrawals in November 2022 due to 3AC and FTX exposure.
historical_instances.description: Genesis bankruptcy — closes out the 2022-23 cascade. By this point most impact was priced in; BTC was already recovering off the FTX-driven lows.
historical_instances.volume_or_flow_change: GBTC discount had blown out during the cascade; began narrowing as the credit crisis cleared.

archetypes.name: Estate Distribution / Repayment Supply Overhang
archetypes.mechanism: Years after an exchange collapses, the bankruptcy estate recovers a large stash of coins and is scheduled to return them to creditors. Because many creditors bought claims cheaply or have held through a 10x+ appreciation, the market PRICES IN the expectation that they will sell on receipt — creating a persistent supply overhang. The reaction is anticipatory and recurring: every on-chain movement of the estate's wallets or every announcement of a repayment date triggers a fresh, usually modest, sell-the-news dip. The actual realized selling is typically smaller and slower than feared (distributions are staggered, some creditors hold), so the overhang fear is often larger than the impact. The other side: the overhang is a known, scheduled, telegraphed event, so it tends to cap upside / add resistance rather than cause a crash. Note that a feared regulatory outcome being RESOLVED with the entity surviving (Binance DOJ settlement Nov 2023) removes existential uncertainty for the broad market, even though the entity's own token can still sell off on the headline.
affected_scope: the named coin held by the estate
leading_signals: Trustee announces a repayment start date or deadline (Mt Gox notices)
On-chain trackers flag movement of dormant estate wallets (e.g., $739M / $1B Mt Gox BTC transfers)
Creditor-claim secondary prices rising (claim buyers front-running)
Coinciding overhang from other forced sellers (German government BTC sales in summer 2024)
archetypes.typical_lag: Anticipatory — dips occur on the announcement/on-chain-movement, often days to weeks BEFORE actual distribution; repayment dates are frequently delayed, resetting the overhang.
archetypes.typical_duration: A persistent multi-month overhang punctuated by sharp 1-3 day dips on each fresh trigger; individual dips fade within days as feared selling fails to fully materialize.
archetypes.typical_magnitude_pct: BTC: roughly -5% to -8% on a given trigger (e.g. the July 2024 ~-6% 24h move); rarely the primary cause of a deep crash, more a drag/resistance.
archetypes.recurrence: Specific to large defunct-exchange estates (Mt Gox is the dominant example, ~140,000 BTC). Recurs each time the estate moves coins or a deadline approaches; the Mt Gox repayment deadline has been repeatedly pushed (most recently to October 2026).

historical_instances.trigger: Mt Gox rehabilitation trustee announced repayments would begin in early July and started distributing on July 5, 2024, returning over 140,000 BTC (worth ~$9B) to creditors a decade after the collapse. Coincided with German government (Saxony) selling seized BTC.
historical_instances.description: Mt Gox creditor repayment overhang materializing — the canonical estate-distribution event. Fear of 140k BTC hitting the market drove an anticipatory selloff.
historical_instances.volume_or_flow_change: Negative spot-ETF flows during the window; liquidation spikes; estate moved $739M-$1B in tracked on-chain transfers.

historical_instances.trigger: Binance settled US DOJ/CFTC/Treasury criminal charges for $4.3B; CEO Changpeng Zhao (CZ) resigned and pleaded guilty. Crucially, Binance SURVIVED as a going concern rather than collapsing.
historical_instances.description: COUNTER-EXAMPLE / non-collapse resolution. A feared existential regulatory threat to the largest exchange was resolved with the exchange intact. The exchange's own token BNB sold off on the headline, but Bitcoin and the broad market were resilient — there was no contagion because Binance remained solvent.

price_impacts.window: intraday Nov 21 (sold off on the settlement news, not a relief rally)

historical_instances.volume_or_flow_change: Brief volume spike; notably NO contagion outflows because the exchange remained solvent and operational — the key differentiator from the FTX/Celsius freezes.

categories.summary: When a crypto protocol, bridge, or exchange is hacked, the asset most directly tied to the victim almost always sells off hard and fast — the native token of an exploited DeFi protocol typically drops 15-45% within hours, while a hacked bridge drags down the L1 ecosystem token behind it 10-25%. The biggest exchange hacks (Bybit's ~$1.5B in Feb 2025) can briefly spook the whole market and push BTC/ETH down a few percent, but those macro shocks tend to mean-revert within days because the loss is socialized by the exchange rather than destroying the underlying network. The reliable, repeatable edge is local and bearish: the closer a token is to the stolen funds (governance token of the hacked protocol > ecosystem L1 > broad market), the larger and more durable the drawdown. 2026 hack regime (DefiLlama): April 2026 was the worst month since the $1.4B Bybit breach (Feb 2025) — ~$606M across 12 incidents in <3 weeks (3.7x Q1), with KelpDAO ($292M) + Drift ($285M) ~95% of it, both Lazarus/DPRK; 2026 YTD ~$771.8M across 47 incidents (+68% YoY frequency, ~1 every 2.9 days), driven by social engineering rather than pure code bugs as DeFi TVL topped $120B. May 2026 cooled sharply to ~$68.3M of exploits (e.g. Verus-Ethereum bridge ~$11.5M, Thorchain ~$10.1M) after April's ~$606M spike - hack severity is lumpy, dominated by a few mega-exploits rather than steady bleed.

archetypes.name: DeFi protocol exploit (governance/native token crash)
archetypes.mechanism: A smart-contract bug (flash loan, reentrancy, oracle/logic flaw) lets an attacker drain a protocol's TVL. The protocol's own governance/native token is the most direct casualty: holders dump because (a) the treasury/insurance backing the token just shrank, (b) future fee revenue collapses as TVL flees, and (c) reflexive fear that more bugs exist. On the other side are panic sellers and forced liquidations of leveraged token positions; buyers are mostly bottom-fishers and the protocol team. The token reprices to a 'damaged-goods' valuation almost instantly.
affected_scope: directly composable protocols (lenders/pools exposed to the victim)
leading_signals: large anomalous outflow from protocol contracts (on-chain netflow spike)
security-firm/whitehat alert tweets before price fully reacts
TVL collapse on DeFiLlama
spike in DEX sell volume of the governance token
founder over-collateralized loan at liquidation risk (e.g. Curve/Aave)
third-party security-risk ratings / 'tokens to avoid' lists flagging the protocol (e.g. Mudrex)
archetypes.typical_lag: immediate to hours (price reacts within minutes of the exploit tx being flagged)
archetypes.typical_duration: days to weeks; partial recovery if funds are returned/socialized, permanent impairment if protocol dies
archetypes.typical_magnitude_pct: -15% to -45% on the hacked protocol's token within 24-72h
archetypes.recurrence: very high — dozens per year across DeFi; the single most frequent crypto bearish catalyst

historical_instances.trigger: KelpDAO rsETH bridge exploit (~$292M) - 116,500 unbacked rsETH minted, dumped as Aave collateral.
historical_instances.description: Attackers exploited a single-signer weakness in KelpDAO's LayerZero bridge to mint ~116,500 unbacked rsETH (~$292M, largest DeFi hack of 2026), then used the stolen liquid-restaking tokens as collateral on Aave V3 to borrow wETH - leaving ~$196M of Aave bad debt and triggering a 'DeFi United' bailout. Aave's own contracts were not compromised, but AAVE fell -17.86% (-26.69% intraday). ETH itself was barely affected (+2.11%) - the damage was DeFi/LRT-specific. Structural lesson: liquid-restaking tokens (LRTs) as cross-protocol collateral are a contagion vector. Resolution: Aave governance temporarily adjusted the rsETH oracle to liquidate the hacker's positions cleanly on Ethereum + Arbitrum, recovering ~$20M and leaving ~10% short of full bad-debt recovery; the 'DeFi United' effort contributed >$320M. DeFi-wide TVL fell >7% in a single day on the breach.
historical_instances.volume_or_flow_change: Aave TVL fell from ~$48.5B to ~$30.7B by Apr 22 (~$15B+ over 4 days); ~$196M Aave bad debt.

historical_instances.trigger: Drift Protocol (Solana) drained ~$285M via durable-nonce admin-takeover; DPRK-linked.
historical_instances.description: Attackers exploited Solana's 'durable nonces' to trick Drift Security Council members into pre-signing dormant transactions that handed over admin control, then whitelisted a worthless fake token (CVT) as collateral and withdrew ~$285M in USDC/SOL/ETH — wiping >50% of Drift's TVL. The 2nd-largest hack of 2026 (the largest until KelpDAO's $292M surpassed it on Apr 18) and the 2nd-largest Solana exploit ever (after Wormhole's $326M, 2022); strong DPRK attribution. Textbook app-specific damage: the DRIFT token cratered (-29.5%, -54.3% intraday) while SOL was essentially flat (-0.46%) — a single-app governance/access-control failure, not an L1 risk. PREDICTIVE-SIGNAL NOTE: Mudrex's April-2026 'cryptos to avoid' list flagged DRIFT (alongside POWER, BERA, Story/IP, and Wormhole/W) for security overhang / unlock pressure / weak adoption right around the exploit - third-party security-risk ratings were a real leading signal, and the flagged cohort broadly underperformed.
historical_instances.volume_or_flow_change: ~$285M drained; >50% of Drift TVL wiped; Drift later floated an airdrop to compensate (controversial).

archetypes.name: Cross-chain bridge exploit (ecosystem L1/token drag-down)
archetypes.mechanism: A bridge holds large pooled collateral and is a fat single point of failure. When it's drained (validator compromise, signature/mint forgery), the loss is huge in absolute terms but the directly tradable casualty is the ecosystem token of the chain the bridge serves (SOL for Wormhole, RON/AXS for Ronin). Wrapped assets become unbacked, so the market sells the L1 token on (a) solvency/trust fear and (b) expectation of capital flight from that ecosystem. Counter-party is often a deep-pocketed backer (Jump for Wormhole) who recapitalizes, which caps the downside and seeds a bounce.
leading_signals: anomalous bridge contract drain (large single mint/withdraw)
wrapped-asset depeg vs underlying
ecosystem TVL outflow
backer/foundation 'we will make users whole' statement (marks the bounce)
spike in L1 perp funding flipping negative
archetypes.typical_lag: immediate to a few hours
archetypes.typical_duration: days; bounce often once a backer recapitalizes, but ecosystem narrative damage can persist weeks
archetypes.typical_magnitude_pct: -10% to -25% on the ecosystem token within 24-48h
archetypes.recurrence: high in 2021-2022 (bridge era); structurally fewer as bridges hardened, but still recurs

historical_instances.trigger: Butter Bridge (MAP Protocol) hash-collision exploit mints 1 quadrillion MAPO.
historical_instances.description: Attackers exploited a hash collision in Butter Bridge's retry-message verification (keccak256(abi.encodePacked) lets different field arrangements hash identically), minting ~1 QUADRILLION MAPO against a legitimate ~208M circulating supply. MAPO collapsed ~96% intraday (~$0.003 to ~$0.0001). Notably the attacker only extracted ~$110K (52.2 ETH) + ~$180K of Uniswap liquidity before the token cratered — the value destruction (-96%) fell on existing holders, not the small realized haul. MAP Protocol paused operations for patch/audit. Canonical infinite-mint bridge exploit: a tiny attacker profit but near-total token-value wipeout.
historical_instances.volume_or_flow_change: 1 quadrillion MAPO minted vs 208M legit supply; ~$290K realized by attacker; protocol paused.

historical_instances.trigger: Hyperbridge forged-MMR-proof exploit mints 1 billion bridged DOT on Ethereum, dumped into Uniswap V4.
historical_instances.description: An attacker forged a Merkle Mountain Range (MMR) proof (missing bounds check) to seize admin rights on Hyperbridge's Ethereum gateway and mint 1 BILLION bridged DOT, dumped in a single tx into a Uniswap V4 pool for 108.2 ETH (~$237K; later revised to ~$2.5M across EVM incentive pools). Native DOT fell only ~4.8% ($1.22->$1.18), ~$20M mcap wiped, >$728K of DOT longs liquidated - because only the *bridged* DOT slice on Ethereum was affected, not the native Polkadot relay chain. The classic infinite-mint pattern (cf. Butter Bridge/MAPO): tiny attacker profit, but here the price damage was contained by shallow bridged-asset liquidity. (5-day measured window -10.5% includes broad mid-April weakness.)
historical_instances.volume_or_flow_change: 1B fake bridged DOT minted; ~$237K (revised ~$2.5M) realized; Uniswap V4 pool drained.

price_impacts.pct_change: depeg / sharp drop on affected wrapped assets

historical_instances.description: Largest DeFi hack of 2021 at the time, but the attacker returned almost all funds within ~2 weeks, so token/market price impact was muted and short-lived relative to the headline size.
historical_instances.volume_or_flow_change: ~$612M drained then almost fully returned by the 'white-hat' attacker by late Aug 2021

archetypes.name: Centralized exchange hack (market-wide sentiment shock, mean-reverting)
archetypes.mechanism: A large CEX has its hot/cold wallets drained. Unlike a protocol token crash, there is usually no single 'victim token' to short — the exchange typically socializes the loss (covers it from reserves or a loan), so the dominant effect is a broad risk-off sentiment shock: BTC/ETH dip, altcoins dip harder, and traders pull liquidity from CEXs. Because the network itself is unharmed and the exchange backstops users, these shocks are the most mean-reverting of the three archetypes — the dip is often bought back within days. The asset literally stolen (e.g. ETH in Bybit) can see transient sell pressure as the hacker launders it.
leading_signals: abnormal large outflow from a known exchange cold wallet
exchange pauses withdrawals
spike in stablecoin inflows / flight to BTC dominance
stolen-asset (e.g. ETH) being moved to mixers/DEXs
exchange CEO 'all funds are safe / loss covered' statement (marks the bottom)
archetypes.typical_lag: immediate (minutes) for the headline; full sentiment effect over hours
archetypes.typical_duration: hours to a few days; rapid recovery once solvency is reassured
archetypes.typical_magnitude_pct: -2% to -6% BTC/ETH same-day; larger drawdowns into following days when a hack compounds an already risk-off macro tape, much of it retraced
archetypes.recurrence: medium — a handful of major CEX hacks per cycle (Mt. Gox, KuCoin, Bybit), but each is large

historical_instances.date_range: theft late-2025; surfaced (ZachXBT) Jan 2026; FBI arrest 2026-03-05

Exposes custody-security gaps in the very wallets feeding the Strategic Bitcoin Reserve — a governance/custody-risk story, not a market mover. categories.summary: Crypto capital constantly rotates between smart-contract platform tokens (Layer-1s like Ethereum, Solana, BNB, Avalanche, Sui, Aptos and Layer-2s like Arbitrum, Optimism, Base) chasing the "next Ethereum killer" narrative. The dominant pattern is reflexive: a chain ships an incentive program, a hot app, a token launch, or a usage milestone, capital and attention flood in, the native token outperforms the market by multiples, and then mean-reverts as the catalyst fades or a newer chain steals the narrative. These moves are large (often +200% to +1500% on the way up, -50% to -90% on the way down), follow identifiable on-chain leading signals (TVL, DEX volume, active addresses, stablecoin inflows), and rotate predictably from "old" leaders to "new" challengers.

archetypes.name: Incentive-Driven Ecosystem Pump (and Fade)
archetypes.mechanism: A chain foundation deploys a large liquidity-mining / DeFi incentive program (e.g. Avalanche Rush $180M, BSC's accelerator funds) that subsidizes yield far above competing chains. Mercenary capital bridges in to farm the rewards, TVL spikes, dApp activity explodes, and the native gas/governance token reflexively rallies as people must buy it to transact and as narrative momentum draws in spot speculators. Who is on the other side: late retail and trend-followers who buy the token at the top to chase the yield narrative, then hold the bag when incentives taper, farmed liquidity exits to the next chain, and TVL/price collapse together. The foundation and early farmers win; latecomers lose.
affected_scope: ecosystem DeFi tokens on that chain
archetypes.typical_lag: days to weeks after the incentive announcement; reflexive run-up builds over 1-3 months
archetypes.typical_duration: 2-4 months of outperformance, then multi-month or multi-year mean reversion once incentives taper
archetypes.typical_magnitude_pct: +200% to +1500% on the L1 token during the incentive window; -70% to -95% on the subsequent unwind
archetypes.recurrence: every cycle - 1-2 major instances per bull market
leading_signals: foundation announces 8-9 figure incentive/grant program
TVL inflow acceleration (TVL_7d_pct_change spike)
DEX volume surge relative to chain baseline
stablecoin net inflow to the chain
rising bridge inflows
native token funding rates turning positive
archetypes.volume_or_flow_change: TVL typically multiplies 5-20x during incentive windows; DEX volume and bridge inflows spike multiples above baseline before reverting

historical_instances.trigger: Avalanche Rush announced - $180M liquidity-mining incentive program bringing Aave and Curve to Avalanche, igniting the subnet/DeFi narrative
historical_instances.description: AVAX ran from roughly $25-26 around the Rush announcement (mid-August 2021) to its all-time high of ~$146.22 on Nov 21 2021 as TVL exploded and mercenary DeFi capital flooded in. Classic incentive-driven reflexive pump. Note: sources disagree on the exact mid-August starting price (estimates range ~$20-43), so the precise percentage is uncertain.
historical_instances.volume_or_flow_change: Avalanche TVL rose from a few hundred million to ~$12B+ during the Rush window

historical_instances.description: BNB surged from ~$44 in January 2021 to an all-time high of ~$690 on May 10 2021 (about +1,460%) as BSC's low-fee DeFi ecosystem absorbed users priced out of Ethereum gas fees.
historical_instances.volume_or_flow_change: BSC daily transactions briefly overtook Ethereum during peak 2021 farming

historical_instances.description: ETH rallied +69.3% from ~$2,595 to ~$4,393 over the summer 2025 rotation back into Ethereum, with an intra-window run-up of +126% to a high near $4,926 (and a -24% max drawdown). Capital rotated from underperforming alt-L1s back into ETH.
historical_instances.volume_or_flow_change: ETH spot ETF inflows and treasury accumulation drove the rotation

archetypes.name: Challenger / 'Next Ethereum-Killer' Revival Rotation
archetypes.mechanism: Capital rotates out of the incumbent leader (usually ETH) into a faster/cheaper challenger L1 that has a fresh catalyst - a killer app, a usage milestone, or a recovery from a crisis low. The challenger's smaller market cap and float make it far more reflexive: a given dollar inflow moves it far more than ETH. Who is on the other side: ETH-maxis and index holders who underweight the challenger and miss the rotation, plus the eventual top-buyers when the narrative peaks. The challenger token tends to massively outperform ETH on the SOL/ETH-type ratio during the rotation, then the ratio mean-reverts once the catalyst saturates or the broader market turns risk-off. Solana's post-FTX revival is the archetypal modern instance.
affected_scope: the challenger L1 token (SOL, SUI, APT, AVAX)
ecosystem tokens on the challenger chain
archetypes.typical_lag: rotation builds over weeks to months as the ratio (challenger/ETH) trends; reaction to a single catalyst (e.g. app going viral) can be immediate
archetypes.typical_duration: a full bull-cycle leg (3-12 months) of outperformance, then ratio mean-reversion
archetypes.typical_magnitude_pct: +200% to +1100% on the challenger over a rotation leg; relative outperformance vs ETH of 30-90% on the ratio
archetypes.recurrence: 1-2 major rotations per cycle; the named challenger changes each cycle (BSC->SOL/AVAX->SOL/SUI)
leading_signals: challenger/ETH price ratio breaking to new highs
challenger DEX volume overtaking ETH for sustained periods
viral dApp launch driving fee revenue
monthly active addresses surpassing rivals
recovery from a crisis/forced-seller low
media 'flippening' headlines (often a late/contrarian signal)
archetypes.volume_or_flow_change: challenger DEX volume can overtake ETH for many consecutive months at peak rotation (Solana DEX volume > ETH for many months in 2024)

historical_instances.trigger: Solana post-FTX revival - SOL recovered as FTX-estate overhang got priced in/auctioned, Visa USDC settlement on Solana (Sep 2023), and the ecosystem proved it survived the FTX collapse
historical_instances.description: SOL rallied from ~$21-22 in early October 2023 to ~$101 by year-end 2023 (about +370% over the quarter). For the full year 2023, SOL ran from ~$24 (Jan 1) to ~$101.50 (Dec 31), roughly +320%. CoinDesk noted a 26% gain in a single week (mid-Oct 2023) despite FTX-sale fears. Capital rotated aggressively into the surviving 'ETH-killer'. (Note: the previously stated '+997%' full-year figure was incorrect.)
historical_instances.volume_or_flow_change: Solana DEX volume and active addresses surged through Q4 2023 into 2024

historical_instances.trigger: Solana memecoin season - Pump.fun launch and Solana becoming the global memecoin trading hub; DEX volume overtook Ethereum for an extended run
historical_instances.description: SOL rose from ~$97 in January 2024 to an all-time high of ~$293-295 on Jan 19 2025 (about +200%) as memecoin retail flow made Solana the most-used chain. Pure reflexive usage-driven rotation. (Note: the prior '~$83 start, +255%' figure overstated the move; SOL opened 2024 near $97.)
historical_instances.volume_or_flow_change: Solana DEX volume exceeded Ethereum's for an extended multi-month run in 2024

historical_instances.trigger: Sui (SUI) Move-language L1 breakout - SUI overtook Aptos in market cap (Sep 2024), DEX volume tripled, claimed 'next Solana' status
historical_instances.description: SUI ran from ~$1.78-1.97 in late September/October 2024 to a December high of ~$4.12 - roughly +110% to +130% over Q4 2024 - as the Move-L1 narrative caught fire and capital rotated into the newest challenger. (Note: the prior '+430%' figure was not supported; the move from the Q4 base to the December peak was closer to +110-130%.)
historical_instances.volume_or_flow_change: Sui DEX trading volume tripled since September 2024

archetypes.name: L2 / New-Chain Token Launch & Airdrop Rotation
archetypes.mechanism: A Layer-2 or new chain that ran without a token (Arbitrum, Optimism, Base, Solana-era pre-token chains) launches or airdrops its governance token. Pre-launch, mercenary 'airdrop farmers' inflate on-chain activity to qualify. At the token-generation event, recipients receive free supply they are inclined to sell (sell-pressure), while the broad market front-runs the rotation into the new ticker. Who is on the other side: airdrop farmers who dump into launch-day liquidity vs. spot speculators and index funds buying the new listing. The token typically peaks near launch or in a first follow-on rally, then bleeds as unlocks and farmed supply hit. Base (Coinbase L2) is a variant where the catalyst flows into the parent equity (COIN) and into speculative 'Base' meme tokens rather than a native chain token.
archetypes.typical_lag: immediate at TGE (price discovery in first hours); first follow-on rally within weeks
archetypes.typical_duration: launch pop fades over days-weeks; multi-month downtrend as unlocks/farmed supply sell off
archetypes.typical_magnitude_pct: wide launch-day price discovery; first-month follow-on rallies of +20% to +30% in single sessions, then multi-month -50%+ bleed
archetypes.recurrence: every major L2/chain TGE - several per cycle
leading_signals: airdrop snapshot / TGE date announced
farmed on-chain activity spike pre-snapshot
exchange listing announcements
large token unlock dates on the vesting schedule
FDV-to-circulating ratio (high = future sell-pressure)
archetypes.volume_or_flow_change: ARB generated ~$1.8B market cap in its first 2 hours; launch-day volumes are extreme then decay

archetypes.name: Rotation Unwind / Challenger Ratio Mean-Reversion
archetypes.mechanism: The mirror image of the revival rotation. After a challenger L1 has massively outperformed (ratio vs ETH at extremes, narrative saturated, media calling a 'flippening'), the catalyst saturates, leverage is crowded long, and the broader market turns risk-off. The high-beta challenger then de-rates far harder than the incumbent: forced liquidations and reflexive deleveraging compound because the challenger has thinner real demand and more mercenary/farmed holders. Who is on the other side: the late top-buyers and over-leveraged longs who get liquidated; disciplined sellers and ETH-relative-value traders win. The challenger/ETH ratio mean-reverts sharply. 2025-2026 Solana underperformance is the live instance.
affected_scope: the previously-leading challenger token (SOL, AVAX, SUI)
archetypes.typical_lag: the ratio top forms over weeks; the unwind accelerates over days once leverage flushes
archetypes.typical_duration: months to a full bear leg of underperformance
archetypes.typical_magnitude_pct: -40% to -90% on the challenger; challenger/ETH ratio reverts 25-50%+ off its high
archetypes.recurrence: follows every challenger rotation - the back half of each cycle
leading_signals: challenger/ETH ratio at multi-year highs with negative momentum divergence
extreme positive funding rates / high open interest on the challenger
'flippening' media saturation
decelerating on-chain growth (active addresses, fees rolling over)
broad-market risk-off (BTC dominance rising)
incentive programs tapering
archetypes.volume_or_flow_change: open interest and funding peak before the unwind; liquidation volume spikes during the flush

historical_instances.trigger: SOL high-beta drawdown in the June-2026 'Bitcoin Season' risk-off — NOT the widely-claimed rally.

A textbook high-beta L1 drawdown amplifying BTC's -13.6% over the same window in a Bitcoin-Season, risk-off tape (see [[bitcoin-dominance-rotation]]). categories.summary: Crypto now trades as a high-beta risk asset tightly coupled to global macro liquidity and the US rates cycle. When dollar liquidity tightens (rate hikes, hot CPI, surging DXY/yen, tariff shocks) crypto sells off harder than equities; when liquidity loosens (rate cuts, soft CPI, falling DXY) it rallies harder. Because the crypto market is structurally over-leveraged, macro shocks routinely trigger forced-liquidation cascades that overshoot the underlying macro move by 2-4x.

archetypes.name: Macro Risk-Off Liquidity Shock
archetypes.mechanism: An exogenous macro shock (surprise hawkish Fed, hot CPI, tariff escalation, global VIX spike) forces a synchronized de-risking across all risk assets. Crypto sits at the far end of the risk curve, so multi-asset funds and leveraged retail dump it first and hardest to raise cash and meet margin. The other side is forced sellers (margin desks, risk-parity/vol-targeting funds shedding the highest-vol sleeve) hitting thin weekend/off-hours order books; passive HODLers and a few dip-buyers absorb at a discount. Sellers 'lose' because they liquidate at panic prices into illiquidity, but they are price-insensitive (compliance/risk-mandated), so the move overshoots fundamentals.
archetypes.typical_lag: immediate to hours (intraday on the headline; equities/futures lead by minutes)
archetypes.typical_duration: 1-7 days for the acute drop; full risk-off regime can persist weeks to months
archetypes.typical_magnitude_pct: BTC -8% to -20% peak-to-trough; alts -20% to -45%
archetypes.recurrence: several times per year; clusters around FOMC days, CPI prints, and geopolitical/tariff headlines
leading_signals: DXY breaking out higher
US 2Y/10Y yields spiking
equity VIX > 25 and rising
Nasdaq futures gapping down
BTC-Nasdaq 30d correlation > 0.5
funding rates very positive (crowded longs) into the event
perp open interest at local highs

historical_instances.trigger: Crypto sells off ~7-9% on USDT-depeg FEARS (no actual depeg) + Tether's retreat from a $20B raise.
historical_instances.description: BTC fell ~9.3% (Feb 1-6 2026, $78.6k->$71.3k; intra-window low $62,822, max DD -20.5%) and the total market dropped to ~$2.42T (from ~$3.5T in late 2025) as retail sentiment cracked on FEARS that USDT (60% of the stablecoin market) might lose its peg, compounded by Tether walking back a reported $20B raise target. ~$775M was liquidated in 24h with ETH/SOL/XRP/DOGE all -6% to -9%. KEY LESSON: USDT did NOT actually depeg (it held ~$1.00 throughout, low $0.9977) - the crash was driven purely by depeg FEAR, a reminder that stablecoin-confidence shocks move markets even with no real depeg.
historical_instances.volume_or_flow_change: ~$775M liquidated in 24h; total mcap to ~$2.42T; USDT held its peg (fear, not fact).

historical_instances.trigger: Trump nominates Kevin Warsh (a known inflation hawk) as Fed Chair; rate fears sink risk assets.
historical_instances.description: Trump named Kevin Warsh his Fed Chair pick on Jan 30 2026. Markets priced him as the worst-case HAWKISH outcome and sold risk: BTC -6% on the day and -14% over the next 10 days; gold -9% (worst session in a decade), silver -30%, XRP -15% the same week. A key trigger of the early-2026 leg of the 2025-26 bear. The paradox: Warsh is crypto-FRIENDLY on regulation (a Bitwise investor) but HAWKISH on rates - markets weighted the rate stance. He replaced Powell on May 15 2026 and BTC still slid to ~$74,190 as the 2y yield held above the Fed target (no rapid cuts expected). Lesson: a 'pro-crypto regulator' is NOT 'dovish' - the Fed chair's RATE stance dominates crypto's liquidity-driven beta.
historical_instances.volume_or_flow_change: Broad risk-off; hawkish-rate repricing across crypto, metals, and equities.

Classic risk-off correlation with collapsing equities. Event predates CoinGecko free-tier window, so figures are web-sourced.
historical_instances.volume_or_flow_change: ~$200B total crypto market cap erased

historical_instances.volume_or_flow_change: >$200M long BTC perps liquidated on BitMEX during the worst phase (broader two-day liquidations widely cited around $700M); record sell volume

archetypes.name: Crypto Leverage Liquidation Cascade (macro-triggered)
archetypes.mechanism: A macro headline pierces a market that is structurally over-leveraged (high perp open interest, positive funding, crowded longs). The initial drop hits liquidation prices, exchanges force-close longs at market, which pushes price lower into thin books, triggering the next tranche of liquidations — a reflexive doom-loop amplified by auto-deleveraging (ADL) and stablecoin/oracle dislocations. The other side is liquidation engines and a handful of opportunistic market-makers buying the air-pocket; over-leveraged longs are wiped out. The crypto-specific wrinkle vs. a plain risk-off shock is that the move is multiples larger than the underlying macro shock because of the leverage stack, and it round-trips fast once forced selling exhausts.
archetypes.typical_lag: minutes to a few hours (mechanical, faster than fundamentals warrant)
archetypes.typical_duration: hours to 2-3 days; sharp V-shaped partial recovery common within 24-72h
archetypes.typical_magnitude_pct: BTC -10% to -16% intraday; alts -25% to -50% intraday wicks
archetypes.recurrence: 1-3 major cascades per year, plus many smaller ones; triggered by macro headlines or exchange events
leading_signals: perp open interest near all-time highs
funding rates persistently positive / elevated
long/short ratio skewed long
estimated leverage ratio rising
thin order-book depth
a binary macro/headline catalyst pending
stablecoin supply not keeping pace with OI

historical_instances.trigger: Trump announces 100% tariffs on Chinese imports via social media — macro headline into a heavily leveraged market
historical_instances.description: Largest liquidation event in crypto history: ~$19B in leveraged positions wiped in 24h. BTC fell ~14% at the worst phase; CoinGecko-measured Oct 9-12 windows: BTC -6.38% close-to-close with -11.01% intra-window drawdown; ETH -18.57% drawdown; SOL -24.12% drawdown. Rebounded sharply by Oct 12 — classic cascade V-recovery.
historical_instances.volume_or_flow_change: ~$19B liquidations in 24h (record); large market-cap recovery by Oct 12

historical_instances.trigger: COVID dash-for-cash hit a leveraged BitMEX-dominated market; cascade amplified the macro shock
historical_instances.description: Within Black Thursday, BTC perps were force-liquidated on BitMEX (>$200M of longs during the worst phase; broader figures around $700M widely cited); the liquidation engine and a brief BitMEX outage helped wick BTC toward ~$3,800-4,000 before a sharp bounce — the prototypical macro-triggered cascade.
historical_instances.volume_or_flow_change: >$200M long BTC perp liquidations on BitMEX during the worst phase; only ~$20M of bids left on the book at one point

historical_instances.trigger: Yen carry unwind margin calls cascaded into crypto perps

historical_instances.volume_or_flow_change: Spike in cross-asset and crypto liquidations

archetypes.name: Macro Liquidity / Risk-On Tailwind
archetypes.mechanism: When the market prices easier policy (a dovish Fed surprise, a rate CUT, a cooler-than-expected CPI, a falling DXY, expanding M2/global liquidity), the discount rate on long-duration risk assets drops and dry powder gets injected. Crypto, as the highest-beta liquidity sponge, rallies hardest. The 'other side' is short sellers and underweight allocators who must cover/chase as liquidity expectations shift; they lose because they fade a regime tailwind. These moves are often anticipatory (price runs into the dovish print) and can fade intraday if the easing was already priced ('buy the rumor, sell the news').
archetypes.typical_lag: immediate on the print, often with anticipatory run-up over prior days/weeks
archetypes.typical_duration: the headline pop is hours-days; the regime tailwind can run weeks-months
archetypes.typical_magnitude_pct: BTC +3% to +12% on the print; multi-week regime moves +20% to +60%
archetypes.recurrence: several FOMC/CPI prints per year; major easing-cycle inflections every few years
leading_signals: DXY rolling over / making lower highs
Fed funds futures pricing cuts
softening CPI/PCE trend
real yields falling
global M2 / central-bank balance sheets expanding
BTC outperforming on up-days (positive beta)
negative funding flipping positive (shorts covering)

historical_instances.trigger: US October CPI came in cooler than expected (7.7% YoY vs 8.0% est; 0.4% MoM vs 0.6% est) — sparked hope of slower Fed hikes
historical_instances.description: BTC, sitting near a 2-year low (~$15.5k) from the FTX blowup, jumped roughly 10% in the hours after the print; Nasdaq +~7%, S&P +~5.5% on the day, 10Y yield fell sharply. Textbook soft-CPI risk-on pop.
historical_instances.volume_or_flow_change: Sharp short-covering volume spike on the print

historical_instances.volume_or_flow_change: Broadening crypto rally following the cut

historical_instances.volume_or_flow_change: Modest; priced-in event

archetypes.name: Banking/Fiat Stress Flight-to-Bitcoin
archetypes.mechanism: When the stress is in the traditional banking/fiat system itself (bank runs, stablecoin-reserve scares, sovereign-currency wobble) rather than a generic risk-off, Bitcoin can decouple and act as a flight-to-quality / 'opt-out-of-the-banking-system' asset. The other side is depositors and stablecoin holders fleeing counterparty risk and bidding BTC as a bearer alternative. This is the rarer, contrarian-bullish macro archetype: the same headline that crushes equities/regional banks lifts BTC. It works when the perceived threat is to fiat plumbing specifically; it fails when the stress is broad liquidity/risk-off (then BTC trades risk-on per archetype 1).
archetypes.typical_lag: hours to a few days (often resolves over a weekend as the banking story develops)
archetypes.typical_duration: days to a couple of weeks while the banking stress is acute
archetypes.typical_magnitude_pct: BTC +15% to +30% over the stress window
archetypes.recurrence: rare — a few times per cycle; requires fiat-system-specific stress
leading_signals: regional-bank equity index falling sharply while BTC holds/rises
a major stablecoin (USDC/DAI) depegging
spike in bank CDS
deposit-flight headlines
BTC-equity correlation breaking down / going negative
stablecoin redemptions and flight to 'unaffected' stablecoins (USDT premium)

historical_instances.trigger: BTC prints a then-record ~$112K as Moody's US credit downgrade (Aaa->Aa1) drives a safe-haven bid, with GENIUS Act Senate passage.
historical_instances.description: Bitcoin hit a then-all-time-high of ~$111,970 on May 22 2025 (intraday ~$112K May 23). Primary driver: Moody's stripped the US of its last Aaa rating (->Aa1) on May 16, and investors bid BTC as a non-sovereign hedge ('debasement trade'). Amplifiers: the Senate advanced the GENIUS Act 66-22 (May 21), JPMorgan said it would let clients buy BTC, and easing US-China trade tensions. Reportedly ~$226M of BTC was liquidated as it broke $112K, ~$214M of it shorts being squeezed (short-squeeze magnitude uncorroborated -> lower confidence). The sovereign-downgrade -> BTC-bid is the distinct macro pattern; $112K was later exceeded ($122,838 Jul 2025, $126,210 Oct 2025).
historical_instances.volume_or_flow_change: Safe-haven inflows on the US downgrade; short squeeze accelerated the break to a new ATH.

historical_instances.trigger: Silicon Valley Bank failure + USDC depeg (Circle disclosed $3.3B reserves stuck at SVB); FDIC backstop announced Mar 12
historical_instances.description: USDC fell to ~$0.87-0.88 over the weekend, then re-pegged after the backstop. Bitcoin decoupled and rallied as a banking-system hedge: ~$19,600 on Mar 10 -> ~$22,386 by Mar 13 (+~18% around the backstop) -> above $28,000 by Mar 20 (~+40% over the window). Flight-to-quality, not risk-off.
historical_instances.volume_or_flow_change: Flight from USDC/DAI/GUSD into USDT (traded above peg); BTC gained ~$26B market cap

historical_instances.trigger: Cyprus banking crisis / bail-in of depositors (haircut on bank deposits)
historical_instances.description: Widely cited early flight-to-Bitcoin episode. Around the Cyprus bail-in BTC was in the low-$40s (~$42 on Mar 7, ~$46 on Mar 9), roughly doubled to ~$90 by end of March (~+119%), then continued a parabolic run to nearly $250 by early-to-mid April before a blow-off — cementing the 'banking-stress hedge' narrative. Older event; magnitude reported, exact start/end points fuzzy.
historical_instances.volume_or_flow_change: Surge in EU-driven BTC buying; narrative-driven inflows

categories.summary: Memecoins are coins with no cash flows or utility whose price is driven entirely by attention, celebrity endorsement, and reflexive speculation. Three repeatable patterns recur: (1) celebrity/influencer pumps where a single tweet or event spikes an existing coin, (2) brand-new "launch-day" tokens (politician coins, pump.fun coins) that go parabolic in hours then collapse 80-98% as insiders dump, and (3) the broader "memecoin season" where froth across the whole sector marks a late-cycle, risk-on top that precedes a market-wide drawdown. For a trader, peak memecoin euphoria has historically been a contrarian sell signal for the entire crypto market, while individual launch tokens are almost always a loss for late buyers.

archetypes.name: Celebrity / Influencer Pump on an Existing Memecoin
archetypes.mechanism: A high-follower individual (Elon Musk, Mark Cuban) tweets or publicly endorses an existing memecoin (DOGE, SHIB). Retail FOMO buyers chase the spike on near-zero float depth, driving a fast parabolic move. The other side is early holders and the influencer's own implicit positioning who distribute into the retail bid. Late buyers consistently lose because the catalyst is a one-off attention shock with no fundamental follow-through; once the news is priced and the tweet stops, liquidity vanishes and price mean-reverts. The 'sell-the-event' dynamic is acute when the catalyst is a scheduled, hyped event (e.g. the SNL appearance) — the run-up front-runs the event and the event itself triggers profit-taking.
archetypes.typical_lag: immediate (minutes to hours after tweet/event)
archetypes.typical_duration: hours to a few days; scheduled-event pumps fade within 24h of the event
archetypes.typical_magnitude_pct: +20% to +600% on the named coin into the catalyst; -20% to -40% sell-the-news reversal within 24-48h
archetypes.recurrence: very high during bull markets — Musk alone moved DOGE on a dozen+ occasions across Jan-May 2021 and again in 2024-2026
leading_signals: spike in social mentions / Google Trends for the coin name
scheduled celebrity media appearance announced in advance
run-up in price + volume in days before a hyped event
influencer follower count vs coin float (thin float amplifies)
Robinhood/exchange app outages from order volume

historical_instances.description: SHIB rallied several hundred percent through October 2021 to an all-time high of ~$0.00008845 on Oct 28 2021, briefly topping a ~$34B market cap.

archetypes.name: Launch-Day Parabolic Token (Politician / Pump.fun / Celebrity Coin)
archetypes.mechanism: A brand-new token launches with almost all supply held by insiders/team and a thin public float. A loud endorsement or hype event (a politician's own announcement, a celebrity, or a viral pump.fun coin) drives a vertical price move within minutes to hours as retail piles in. Insiders and sniper bots that bought pre-announcement (sometimes seconds before) sell into the retail bid. Late buyers almost always lose because the float-unlock and insider distribution overwhelms demand once attention peaks. This is a near-pure wealth transfer from late retail to insiders/snipers; the LIBRA case showed ~86% of participants lost money. Directional bias is bullish only for the first minutes/hours and sharply bearish thereafter.
archetypes.typical_lag: the pump is immediate (minutes); the dump follows within hours to days
archetypes.typical_duration: peak within 24h; 80-98% collapse over days to weeks
archetypes.typical_magnitude_pct: +1000% to +10000% in the first hours, then -80% to -98% from peak within weeks
archetypes.recurrence: extremely high during froth windows — pump.fun launched thousands daily in late 2024 / early 2025; politician coins clustered Jan-Feb 2025
leading_signals: insider/sniper wallets buying seconds-to-minutes before public announcement
pct of supply held by team/top wallets
no liquidity lock / no vesting
concentrated holder distribution (top-10 wallet share)
explosive first-hour volume with vertical price
official figure directly endorsing (legitimacy halo)

historical_instances.volume_or_flow_change: insider wallets bought ~$2.6M ~2.5 min before announcement, multi-million-dollar payday

Insiders extracted roughly $87-107M; ~86% of investors lost money. Triggered a federal fraud investigation of Milei.

The exact single-day percent is not independently corroborated.
historical_instances.volume_or_flow_change: pump.fun ~$15M/day record revenue early Jan 2025; launchpad memecoins a large share of memecoin mcap

archetypes.name: Memecoin Season as a Late-Cycle Risk-On Top Signal
archetypes.mechanism: When speculative capital rotates down the risk curve into the lowest-quality assets (memecoins), it signals that the marginal buyer is exhausted and dry powder is being spent on pure gambling rather than on BTC/large-caps. Peak memecoin froth — record launchpad revenue, parabolic joke coins, politician coins, celebrity coins — has repeatedly coincided with or immediately preceded the cycle top in the broader crypto market. The other side: smart money / early holders distribute large-cap and memecoin positions into euphoric retail. Once froth peaks, the whole sector deflates 75-80% and capital consolidates back into BTC, dragging alts down harder than BTC. Direction is bearish for the forward 1-6 month return of the broad market once the froth-peak signal fires.
archetypes.typical_lag: froth peak leads the broad top by days to a few weeks
archetypes.typical_duration: sector drawdown plays out over months; -75% to -80% peak-to-trough in memecoin market cap
archetypes.typical_magnitude_pct: memecoin sector market cap -75% to -80% from cycle peak; broad market correction of -20% to -50%+
archetypes.recurrence: once per major cycle (2021, late-2024/early-2025); froth peaks recurred each bull phase
leading_signals: memecoin sector market cap at record + as a rising share of total crypto mcap
launchpad (pump.fun) daily revenue at record highs
politician/celebrity coins launching (peak novelty, then novelty exhaustion)
joke coins (Fartcoin) at ATHs
memecoin share of total mcap above a high threshold
negative funding flips / BTC dominance starting to rise

historical_instances.description: Memecoins rebounded from 2025 lows, adding >$8B to reach ~$47B market cap by Jan 5 2026 (+~30% in days; sector volume ~$9.2B, up ~87% YoY in 2026). The spikes were violent - intra-month MAX RUNUPS: PEPE +77%, BONK +76%, FLOKI +55%, SHIB +40%, DOGE +31% (DOGE briefly broke a year-long downtrend). But the froth fully ROUND-TRIPPED within January: every name closed the month NEGATIVE (DOGE -12.9%, FLOKI -13.5%, BONK -5.9%, SHIB -3.7%, PEPE ~flat) with -33% to -47% max drawdowns. A textbook risk-on memecoin spike-and-fade and an early-2026 froth top that preceded the broad 2025-26 bear. OFFICIAL TRUMP remains a political-beta play that pops on Trump-crypto-positive news. DATA-INTEGRITY NOTE: a separately-claimed 'DOGE +8% June-2026 alt-season rally' is FALSE - DOGE was -12.5% (Jun 1-4 2026) in the risk-off crash; June 2026 was Bitcoin Season, not alt-season.

categories.summary: When a crypto platform finally launches its token (a "TGE" = token generation event), it usually airdrops a chunk of supply to the users who farmed it for points. The dominant, highly repeatable pattern is "sell-the-news": pre-launch hype peaks, the token opens high, and airdrop recipients plus farmers immediately dump for free profit, so the token falls 20-60% within hours to days (STRK, W, ZK, EIGEN, SCR, BLAST all did this). A rarer pattern is the launch-pump-and-hold, where a token with real product revenue and no VC overhang keeps rising for weeks (HYPE is the standout). Separately, the points-farming meta is a leading bullish signal: when a big launch is anticipated, mercenary capital floods the chain/protocol beforehand (deposits, TVL, fees), and that inflow tends to drain right after the airdrop ("farm-and-dump").

archetypes.name: TGE / Airdrop Sell-the-News Dump
archetypes.mechanism: On the launch day a large slice of supply (typically 7-20% of total) lands in the wallets of airdrop recipients and points farmers who paid little or nothing for it. Their cost basis is effectively zero, so the rational move is to sell immediately and lock in the gift. The buyers on the other side are pre-launch hype chasers, perp pre-market longs, and exchange retail FOMO who bought the top expecting a continued pump. Pre-launch perp/IOU markets and 'guess the price' threads set anchor prices far above what airdrop sellers will defend, so the open is the high. Farmers keep winning because the protocol's own marketing creates the demand that absorbs their supply exactly at peak. Sybil farmers amplify this: thin anti-sybil measures (STRK, ZK, SCR) mean a few whales/farmers control a disproportionate share and dump in size.
archetypes.typical_lag: immediate (minutes to first 24-72h)
archetypes.typical_duration: weeks to months; many never reclaim debut price (W, ZK, SCR, BLAST, STRK, IP)
archetypes.typical_magnitude_pct: -20% to -60% on the named coin from open within 1-7 days; -50% to -90% peak-to-trough over weeks
archetypes.recurrence: Near-universal for VC-backed L1/L2 and infra airdrops 2023-2026. The default expectation for any farmed airdrop.
leading_signals: pre-market/IOU perp price far above implied airdrop FDV
high points-farming TVL with mercenary (non-sticky) capital
low or absent anti-sybil controls / no per-wallet claim cap
large % of supply unlocked at TGE to farmers vs locked to insiders
spike in exchange inflows (deposit netflow z-score) right as claims open
community complaints about allocation fairness pre-launch

archetypes.name: Launch-Pump-and-Hold (Fair-Launch / Revenue-Backed)
archetypes.mechanism: The rare inverse of sell-the-news. When a token launches with (a) NO VC/private-investor allocation to dump, (b) a product with real, demonstrable revenue/usage, and (c) supply concentrated in users who believe in continued upside, the airdrop sell pressure is absorbed and the token keeps climbing for weeks. The buyers (exchange-listing retail, momentum funds, ecosystem believers) outweigh sellers because the float is small and the narrative is 'this is undervalued vs its fees.' Holders win because there is no insider cliff hanging over them and the protocol prints fees that justify the cap. This is fragile and reverts to the broad market eventually (see HYPE's later drawdown), but the launch window is structurally bullish.
archetypes.typical_lag: hours to days for initial pump; momentum persists weeks
archetypes.typical_duration: 2-8 weeks of outperformance, then reverts to market beta
archetypes.typical_magnitude_pct: +50% to +700% on the named coin within weeks of launch
archetypes.recurrence: Rare. Requires no-VC fair-ish distribution + real revenue. HYPE is the canonical case; most launches do NOT qualify.
leading_signals: zero private investor / VC allocation (no insider cliff)
protocol has real, measurable revenue/fees pre-launch
large % of recipients NOT depositing to exchanges (low sell intent)
small initial float / high reserved-for-future emissions
rising open interest with positive funding but spot leading perps

archetypes.name: Points-Farming Meta - Pre-Launch Inflow & Post-Airdrop Outflow
archetypes.mechanism: Before a TGE, protocols run 'points' programs that promise a future airdrop weighted by deposits/usage. This pulls in mercenary capital: TVL, deposits, fees, and on-chain activity surge as farmers chase the largest expected allocation - a dry-powder-injection into the chain/protocol and often its native gas/ecosystem token. The capital is non-sticky. Once the snapshot/eligibility window closes or the airdrop lands, farmers withdraw and rotate to the next farm, draining TVL and on-chain activity (the 'farm-and-dump' at the protocol level, distinct from selling the token itself). The other side: the protocol effectively rents liquidity at the cost of future token dilution; late farmers who buy ecosystem tokens expecting sustained activity lose when the activity evaporates. The meta itself peaked/decayed around the EIGEN airdrop (Oct 2024), widely called the 'demise of points.'
archetypes.typical_lag: inflow builds over weeks/months pre-snapshot; outflow within days of snapshot/claim
archetypes.typical_duration: TVL/activity reversion over days to weeks post-airdrop
archetypes.typical_magnitude_pct: protocol TVL/activity swings of -30% to -70% post-snapshot; ecosystem-token effect small unless airdrop is large
archetypes.recurrence: Was dominant 2023-mid-2024 (EigenLayer, Blast, restaking/LRTs, Solana farms); structurally decayed after EIGEN disappointment but recurs around every major anticipated launch (Plasma deposits 2025).
leading_signals: announced points program without confirmed tokenomics
TVL/deposit growth far outpacing organic usage (fees-per-TVL falling)
concentration of deposits in 'farmable' assets right before rumored snapshot
on-chain transaction count rising while unique-user retention is low
social mentions of 'farming' + leverage loops (recursive LRT deposits)

archetypes.name: Revenue-funded buyback flywheel (usage -> token demand)
archetypes.mechanism: A protocol routes the bulk of its trading-fee revenue into continuous open-market buybacks of its own token, turning exchange usage directly into automatic, price-insensitive token demand. Hyperliquid's Assistance Fund is the canonical case: ~99% of perps+spot revenue buys HYPE block-by-block, with >$1.16B bought back since launch. This is a supply-restriction + dry-powder demand flywheel that can DECOUPLE the token from the broad market while volume is high. The reflexive catch: buybacks scale with volume, so the flywheel reverses in a volume downturn (Hyperliquid's quarterly buybacks already fell from $316.8M in Q3-2025 to $192.3M in Q1-2026, -40%). The people on the other side are shorts fading a token that has a structural, mechanical bid as long as the venue is used.
archetypes.typical_magnitude_pct: can drive multi-month outperformance/decoupling (HYPE +82.9% in May 2026 to an ATH while majors fell); magnitude tracks fee revenue
archetypes.typical_lag: continuous (block-by-block); compounds over weeks-months
archetypes.typical_duration: persists while trading volume/fees stay high; reverses on a volume downturn
archetypes.recurrence: structural/ongoing per protocol with a buyback mandate; a growing 2025-2026 tokenomics meta
leading_signals: protocol fee revenue + buyback wallet on-chain accumulation
token outperformance vs BTC (decoupling)
perp/spot volume trend (the flywheel's fuel)
FDV-to-annualized-revenue multiple
social dominance / attention spikes

historical_instances.trigger: HYPE hits an ATH ~$75.51 (Jun 2 2026), decoupling UPWARD while BTC/ETH/SOL crash - driven by the Assistance Fund buyback, not the ETF.
historical_instances.description: Hyperliquid's Assistance Fund (>$1.16B of fee-funded HYPE buybacks since launch, ~99% of revenue) drove HYPE to an all-time high of ~$75.51 on Jun 2 2026 (+82.9% in May, into the top 10 at ~$16B+ mcap), DECOUPLING upward while BTC fell ~13.6% and ETH/SOL crashed over the same window. Analysts widely credited the buyback flywheel - NOT the new Bitwise/21Shares HYPE spot ETFs (launched ~May 14 2026) - as the real driver ('the ETF had almost nothing to do with it'). HYPE briefly rivaled SOL on FDV. Reflexive risk: buybacks scale with volume (already -40% QoQ into Q1-2026); a volume downturn reverses the bid. HYPE then pulled back -7.35% Jun 1-4 with the broad tape.
