archetypes.name: Pre-IPO / synthetic-asset perp oracle-failure flash crash
archetypes.mechanism: New on-chain perp venues list contracts on illiquid, no-public-benchmark underlyings (pre-IPO names, synthetic equities) priced by a single third-party oracle. When the oracle mishandles a corporate action (stock split, dividend) or thin liquidity, the pricing engine treats an artifact as a real move and liquidates leveraged positions in minutes. The losers are high-leverage retail with tiny margins; the lesson is that pre-IPO/synthetic perps carry severe oracle and corporate-action risk distinct from the underlying token's health.
archetypes.typical_magnitude_pct: -45% to -80% in minutes on the affected contract; underlying platform token typically unaffected
archetypes.typical_lag: instant (single-block oracle print)
archetypes.typical_duration: minutes (often partial recovery once the bad print clears)
archetypes.recurrence: rising as pre-IPO/prediction/synthetic-equity perps proliferate (Hyperliquid Ventuals, Bitget preSPCX, etc.)
leading_signals: single-oracle dependency for an illiquid underlying
upcoming corporate action (split/dividend) on the referenced asset
thin order-book depth + high open leverage
low median position margin (retail-skewed)

historical_instances.trigger: Hyperliquid's SPACEX-USDH pre-IPO perp flash-crashes -45% on a faulty oracle mishandling SpaceX's 5-for-1 split.
historical_instances.description: Hyperliquid's SPACEX-USDH pre-IPO perpetual (via Ventuals) plunged from an open of $2,277 to $1,254 (-45%) in ~30 minutes before recovering to ~$2,169. Cause: the Notice.co oracle fed SpaceX's 5-for-1 stock split into the pricing engine as an ~80% drop. ~$1.51M was liquidated across 1,393 positions (405 users; median margin just $31 - high-leverage retail). Bitget's preSPCX fell ~80% on the same corporate action. The HYPE token itself was unaffected (held near its highs) - a contained microstructure/oracle failure, not a protocol risk.
historical_instances.volume_or_flow_change: $1.51M liquidated; 1,393 positions / 405 users; median margin ~$31.

categories.summary: When governments approve new crypto investment products (like ETFs) or signal friendlier rules, prices tend to move in predictable ways. Pre-announced approvals often pump on rumor then dump on the actual news ("sell the news"), while genuine surprise policy shifts — a pro-crypto election win, a coin being named in a reserve, or a hostile regulator leaving — can trigger violent, durable rallies, with smaller altcoins moving far more than Bitcoin. Legislative frameworks (stablecoin/market-structure laws) tend to re-rate the specific sector they touch (e.g. Ethereum and stablecoin infrastructure) more than Bitcoin itself. By 2025-2026 the market entered a 'policy-fatigue' regime: after the Nov-2024 election and Mar-2025 reserve-naming, incremental legislative progress (committee markups, bill introductions, implementation rulemakings, state reserve actions, new-product approvals) produced little or no isolable spot move — broad market regime dominated the headline.

archetypes.name: Pre-priced approval 'sell-the-news' then structural re-rating
archetypes.mechanism: A long-telegraphed regulatory approval of an investment vehicle (spot ETF) gets priced in during the rumor/anticipation phase. On the actual approval, event-driven traders and pre-positioned funds take profit into the news, producing a short-term dump even though the long-term effect is structurally bullish (new persistent inflow channel). The people 'losing' short-term are late retail buyers chasing the headline; the structural winners are holders who survive the dip as ETF inflows create steady new demand (supply absorption) over the following months.
leading_signals: rising implied volatility / funding rates into the decision date
futures-basis blowout (annualized basis >15-20%)
spike in 'days_to_decision' search/social volume
exchange BTC balances declining pre-event
Polymarket/Kalshi approval-odds approaching ~100%
archetypes.typical_lag: immediate (minutes-to-hours on the event); structural inflow effect over weeks-months
archetypes.typical_duration: sell-off 1-3 weeks; structural re-rating 1-9 months
archetypes.typical_magnitude_pct: -14% to -19% drawdown on the named asset in the 2 weeks post-approval; +50% to +150% over the following 6-12 months
archetypes.recurrence: once per product launch (BTC spot ETF, ETH spot ETF); recurs as new vehicles get approved

historical_instances.trigger: SEC approves 11 US spot Bitcoin ETFs; trading begins Jan 11.
historical_instances.description: SEC approves 11 US spot Bitcoin ETFs; trading begins Jan 11, 2024. Classic 'sell the news' after a multi-month rumor rally: BTC peaked near $49k on Jan 11 then fell to a Jan 23 trough of ~$39,567 before a structural re-rating to new ATHs by March.
historical_instances.volume_or_flow_change: Day-one ETF trading volume ~$4.6B across 11 funds; net inflows turned strongly positive within weeks, GBTC outflows offset by IBIT/FBTC inflows.

historical_instances.trigger: SEC approves spot Ethereum ETF 19b-4 filings (May 23); spot ETH ETFs begin trading July 23, 2024.
historical_instances.description: SEC approves spot Ethereum ETF 19b-4 filings (May 23, 2024); spot ETH ETFs begin trading July 23. ETH ran up ~25-30% from ~$3,000 (May 17) toward ~$3,800-3,900 on approval expectations, then sold the actual July launch on weak first-month flows and ETHE outflows.

price_impacts.window: May 17 (~$3,000) to May 23 (~$3,800-3,900) on approval anticipation

historical_instances.volume_or_flow_change: Spot ETH ETFs saw weaker first-month flows than BTC; persistent ETHE (Grayscale) outflows pressured price post-launch.

archetypes.name: Pro-crypto policy regime shift (election / executive pivot)
archetypes.mechanism: A genuine, hard-to-fully-anticipate shift in the regulatory regime — a pro-crypto candidate winning, or an executive order signaling state endorsement — reprices the entire asset class's regulatory risk premium downward. Shorts and underweight institutions are forced to chase; the move is broad and reflexive. Altcoins exhibit high beta because they carried the most regulatory/securities-law overhang, so de-risking benefits them disproportionately. Those on the other side are bearish macro funds and regulators-friendly positioning caught offside.
leading_signals: prediction-market odds shifting toward the pro-crypto outcome
crypto-linked equities (COIN, MSTR) front-running
rising open interest with positive funding
campaign/policy statements (e.g. Bitcoin Conference promises)
archetypes.typical_lag: immediate (overnight) and continuing for days-to-weeks
archetypes.typical_duration: weeks to a few months of sustained trend
archetypes.typical_magnitude_pct: BTC +25% to +55% over 1-6 weeks; high-beta alts +50% to +150%+
archetypes.recurrence: rare / episodic — tied to major political or executive events

historical_instances.trigger: Trump wins US presidential election on an explicitly pro-crypto platform.
historical_instances.description: Donald Trump wins the US presidential election on an explicitly pro-crypto platform (crypto council, strategic BTC stockpile, fire Gensler). Broad regulatory re-rating drove BTC up ~28% in the first week and to its first $100k+ print by Dec 17, with Musk-linked DOGE up triple digits.

price_impacts.window: Election Day Nov 5 (~$69,539 close) to ~Nov 13 week
Nov 5 close (~$69,539) to Dec 17 peak (~$106k, first >$100k)
election +24h (later much larger on Gensler exit)

historical_instances.volume_or_flow_change: Record spot+perp volumes; spot BTC ETF inflows surged to multi-billion weekly; COIN and MSTR equities rallied sharply.

historical_instances.trigger: Trump Truth Social post naming BTC, ETH, XRP, SOL, ADA for a US 'Crypto Strategic Reserve'.
historical_instances.description: Trump Truth Social post naming XRP, SOL, ADA (then adding BTC, ETH) for a US 'Crypto Strategic Reserve'. Surprise endorsement of specific named coins triggered a weekend pump strongest in the smaller named alts (ADA +60-69% intraday) and more muted in BTC. Posts sparked a ~$300B crypto market-cap rally.
historical_instances.volume_or_flow_change: Sharp thin-weekend volume spike; gains in named alts largely round-tripped within days as detail (no new buying) disappointed.

historical_instances.trigger: Trump signs EO establishing the US Strategic Bitcoin Reserve.
historical_instances.description: Trump signs EO establishing the US Strategic Bitcoin Reserve. 'Sell the news': reserve funded with already-seized BTC, no new open-market purchases promised, so BTC fell several percent and the Mar 2 reserve-naming alts gave back most gains.
historical_instances.volume_or_flow_change: Reserve-naming alts (XRP, SOL, ADA) gave back most of the Mar 2 weekend gains as 'no new buying' disappointed the market.

archetypes.name: Idiosyncratic regulatory de-risking of a named coin
archetypes.mechanism: A regulatory overhang specific to one asset is lifted or expected to lift — a hostile regulator departs, a lawsuit settles, an asset is endorsed, or an accounting penalty is removed. Because the overhang was priced as a structural discount, its removal causes a sharp, asset-specific re-rating that decouples from the broad market. The losers are shorts and those who avoided the asset precisely because of the regulatory risk; the winners are holders who bet the overhang would lift. XRP (Ripple/SEC) is the canonical case.
leading_signals: news/rumor of regulator departure or settlement
litigation docket dates
options skew normalizing for the named asset
the asset's discount-to-peers narrowing
social-volume spike on the specific ticker
archetypes.typical_lag: immediate (hours)
archetypes.typical_duration: days to weeks; can mark a durable regime change for that asset
archetypes.typical_magnitude_pct: +20% to +30%+ on the named coin in 24-72h, often with low BTC correlation
archetypes.recurrence: episodic per asset; XRP repeatedly re-rated on Ripple-SEC milestones

historical_instances.trigger: Gary Gensler announces SEC resignation (effective Jan 20, 2025).
historical_instances.description: Gary Gensler announces his SEC resignation (effective Jan 20, 2025). Markets read it as the end of the Ripple-SEC overhang; XRP spiked ~25% in 24h to a 3-year high (~$1.43) in a highly idiosyncratic move vs BTC.
historical_instances.volume_or_flow_change: XRP volume and open interest spiked to multi-year highs; the move was highly idiosyncratic vs BTC.

historical_instances.trigger: SEC issues SAB 122, rescinding SAB 121.
historical_instances.description: SEC issues SAB 122, rescinding SAB 121. Removes the balance-sheet liability rule that blocked banks from offering crypto custody — a structural de-risk for institutional custody, though little immediate spot move as it was part of an expected post-inauguration package.
historical_instances.volume_or_flow_change: Structural positive for bank custody adoption (BNY, etc.); little immediate spot price move.

historical_instances.trigger: FASB ASU 2023-08 fair-value crypto accounting takes effect.
historical_instances.description: FASB ASU 2023-08 fair-value crypto accounting takes effect (fiscal years beginning after Dec 15, 2024). Removes the impairment-only 'down-only' accounting drag for corporate BTC holders like MicroStrategy, enabling the corporate bitcoin-treasury strategy to scale.
historical_instances.volume_or_flow_change: Enabled corporate bitcoin-treasury accumulation (MSTR through 2024-2025); supportive of persistent corporate BTC demand.

archetypes.name: Legislative framework passage (stablecoin / market-structure)
archetypes.mechanism: Passage (or near-passage) of a sweeping crypto law clarifies which assets fall under SEC vs CFTC and legitimizes a sub-sector (stablecoins, settlement layers). The re-rating concentrates in the sector the law touches: stablecoin/payments infrastructure and the chains that host most stablecoin/tokenization activity (Ethereum) benefit far more than Bitcoin, which is already 'regulatorily clean.' Those on the other side are skeptics who doubted Congress could pass crypto law and were underweight ETH/alts; the winners rotate into the freshly-legitimized sector.
leading_signals: congressional calendar / committee markup dates
whip counts and procedural-vote outcomes
ETH/BTC ratio turning up
stablecoin-issuer and DeFi token relative strength
ETH ETF inflow acceleration
archetypes.typical_lag: days (often building over the legislative-debate week)
archetypes.typical_duration: 1-4 weeks of sector outperformance; can extend if law actually signed
archetypes.typical_magnitude_pct: Sector/ETH +20% to +30% while BTC roughly flat to +5%
archetypes.recurrence: per major bill (FIT21, GENIUS, CLARITY); recurs as the legislative pipeline advances

historical_instances.trigger: Senate Banking Committee advances the Digital Asset Market Clarity (CLARITY/DAMA) Act in a 15-9 bipartisan vote.
historical_instances.description: Senate Banking Committee advances the CLARITY Act 15-9 (Dems Gallego and Alsobrooks crossing over), the most significant market-structure milestone since FIT21; it heads to reconciliation with the Senate Ag 'Digital Commodity Intermediaries Act' and the House bill. The landmark vote produced NO bullish reaction — BTC fell -5.79% and ETH -8.81% over the window as the broad May-2026 downtrend dominated. The clearest example of the 2025-2026 'policy-fatigue' regime: even a genuine bipartisan legislative breakthrough is priced as noise against the prevailing regime.

price_impacts.window: May 12-18 2026 (fell despite the bullish milestone; regime dominated)

historical_instances.volume_or_flow_change: No positive flow response; market in a broad downtrend.

historical_instances.trigger: Treasury FinCEN + OFAC issue a joint proposed rule implementing the GENIUS Act's AML/sanctions requirements for stablecoin issuers.
historical_instances.description: FinCEN and OFAC issue a joint proposed rule (Federal Register 2026-06963) implementing the GENIUS Act: permitted payment stablecoin issuers (PPSIs) are treated as financial institutions under the Bank Secrecy Act and must run risk-based AML and sanctions-compliance programs (with transaction-blocking). Reserve/capital/liquidity rules are tasked to the OCC, Fed, FDIC and NCUA. A GENIUS-implementation rulemaking that formalizes stablecoin oversight but produced no isolable spot move (BTC +2.96% over the window is noise).
historical_instances.volume_or_flow_change: Structural compliance burden for stablecoin issuers; no immediate price/flow event.

historical_instances.trigger: US House 'Crypto Week'; CLARITY + GENIUS Acts; Trump signs GENIUS Act July 18.
historical_instances.description: US House 'Crypto Week' debates and passes the CLARITY (market structure) and GENIUS (stablecoin) Acts; Trump signs GENIUS into law July 18, 2025. ETH re-rated +25.16% (intra-window run-up +30.35%) as the dominant stablecoin/settlement chain while BTC stayed flat (+0.64%).
historical_instances.volume_or_flow_change: Spot ETH ETF inflows accelerated sharply during this window; ETH/BTC ratio broke out as capital rotated into the stablecoin-settlement narrative.

historical_instances.trigger: US Senate passes the GENIUS Act (first federal payment-stablecoin framework).
historical_instances.description: US Senate passes the GENIUS Act, the first federal payment-stablecoin framework. Reaction muted on the vote itself — BTC drifted slightly down (~-2%) holding ~$104-107k pending the Fed; the larger price leg came on the July House passage + signing.
historical_instances.volume_or_flow_change: Positive sentiment for stablecoin issuers/exchanges; price reaction deferred to the House passage + presidential signature in July.

historical_instances.trigger: EU MiCA stablecoin rules apply; non-compliant stablecoin (USDT) delistings follow.
historical_instances.description: EU MiCA stablecoin (EMT/ART) rules apply Jun 30 2024; CASP rules Dec 30 2024. Drove non-compliant stablecoin (USDT) delistings across EU venues into Q1 2025 — a supply/liquidity-restriction and market-share reallocation toward USDC/EURC, not a token price pump.

price_impacts.window: Q4 2024 to Q2 2025 (USDT trading on EU venues)

historical_instances.volume_or_flow_change: USDT/EUR order-book depth collapsed on EU exchanges; USDC/EURC depth expanded as MiCA-authorized issuers gained share.

historical_instances.trigger: US House passes FIT21 market-structure bill.
historical_instances.description: US House passes FIT21 (Financial Innovation and Technology for the 21st Century Act), the first major crypto market-structure bill, with bipartisan support. Sentiment-positive for the regulatory-clarity narrative; coincided with ETH ETF approval optimism the next day, making a clean standalone price move hard to isolate.

price_impacts.pct_change: +20% to +30% week-of (driven mainly by ETH-ETF approval anticipation, not FIT21)

historical_instances.volume_or_flow_change: Hard to isolate from the concurrent ETH ETF approval catalyst; FIT21 itself produced no clean standalone price move.

archetypes.name: Sovereign / state strategic-reserve accumulation
archetypes.mechanism: A government (federal or US state) signals or executes strategic Bitcoin accumulation. Announcements and enabling bills are sentiment events; actual purchases create real demand. The structural bull case is large, sustained sovereign buying that withdraws float (a sovereign-scale analog of the corporate-treasury flywheel — dry-powder plus supply-restriction). In practice 2025-2026 per-event price impact is negligible: authorized/executed amounts are tiny versus market depth (Texas' first buy was ~$5M), purchases are telegraphed, and bills face long odds. The 'other side' is skeptics doubting follow-through; the structural winners are holders if sovereign demand ever scales.
leading_signals: state/federal strategic-reserve bill introductions and committee markups
state-treasurer investment mandates / appropriations
actual on-chain state or Treasury BTC purchases (size vs daily volume)
count of jurisdictions with enacted reserve authority
authorized annual purchase size vs market depth
archetypes.typical_lag: immediate but typically negligible on the headline
archetypes.typical_duration: per-event minutes-to-hours; structural over years only if buying actually scales
archetypes.typical_magnitude_pct: per-event not isolable (<~3%, lost in market noise); structurally bullish only with sustained large sovereign buying
archetypes.recurrence: rising 2025-2026 — federal SBR EO (Mar 2025), Texas (enacted + first buy), Arizona & New Hampshire (enabling laws, no buys), ARMA federal bill

historical_instances.trigger: Texas Gov. Greg Abbott signs SB 21 creating the Texas Strategic Bitcoin Reserve.
historical_instances.description: Texas Gov. Abbott signs SB 21 (Senate 25-5 in Mar 2025, House 101-42 in May 2025) creating the first US state strategic Bitcoin reserve; the reserve may buy crypto with a >=$500B 24-month average market cap (BTC-only at the time). No isolable price reaction — BTC drifted within mid-2025 chop.
historical_instances.volume_or_flow_change: Symbolic/structural; no measurable flow event (purchases came months later).

historical_instances.trigger: Texas executes a ~$5M Bitcoin purchase — first US state to buy BTC with state funds.
historical_instances.description: Texas executes a ~$5M BTC purchase under SB 21, becoming the first US state to actually hold Bitcoin with state funds. The amount is immaterial versus market depth; BTC's ~+6% over the surrounding window was a recovery bounce off the November 2025 drawdown low, not Texas-driven. Symbolic milestone for the sovereign-adoption narrative.

price_impacts.window: Nov 24-Dec 2 2025 (market recovery bounce, NOT Texas-driven; $5M immaterial)

historical_instances.volume_or_flow_change: ~$5M actual purchase — negligible vs ~$20-40B BTC daily spot volume.

historical_instances.trigger: Reps. Nick Begich (R-AK) & Jared Golden (D-ME) introduce the American Reserve Modernization Act (ARMA).
historical_instances.description: Bipartisan ARMA introduced — a revised BITCOIN Act that would authorize the US Treasury to buy up to 200,000 BTC/year for 5 years (1,000,000 BTC goal) with a 20-year mandatory hold. No bullish reaction: BTC fell ~2% over the window amid a broader May-2026 downtrend, a textbook policy-fatigue non-event.

price_impacts.window: May 19-26 2026 (no positive reaction; market in downtrend)

historical_instances.volume_or_flow_change: Bill introduction only — no flow; long legislative odds.

archetypes.name: Regulated domestic access / new-product expansion
archetypes.mechanism: A regulator clears a new onshore product or venue (perpetual futures, leverage, new derivatives, prediction-market preemption) that widens domestic access, market depth, and legitimacy. The effect is structural — onshoring volume and bringing global-style products under US oversight — not a clean spot catalyst on the approval date. The 'other side' is offshore venues losing US share. For backtesting, the tradeable read-through is in domestic derivatives open interest, onshore-vs-offshore funding, and the listed exchange's (COIN) equity, more than in spot.
leading_signals: DCM self-certification filings (CFTC non-objection windows)
CFTC/SEC product listing approvals
appellate preemption rulings on event contracts
onshore vs offshore perp funding/OI divergence
archetypes.typical_lag: structural — builds over weeks-to-months, not a date-stamped spot move
archetypes.typical_duration: persistent (market-structure change)
archetypes.typical_magnitude_pct: no clean spot move on the event; structural depth/liquidity gain
archetypes.recurrence: ongoing as new products/venues are approved

historical_instances.trigger: Coinbase launches the first CFTC-regulated crypto perpetual futures for US traders.
historical_instances.description: Coinbase Financial Markets goes live with CFTC-regulated nano BTC and ETH perpetual futures (5-year-dated, up to 10x leverage) — the first onshore US retail perps, after self-certifying Jun 26 with no CFTC objection. Market-structure milestone (90% of global crypto derivatives volume is perps) with no isolable BTC reaction (-0.94% over the window); ETH's +6.91% same week is attributable to the concurrent House 'Crypto Week'/GENIUS catalyst, not the perps launch.

price_impacts.window: Jul 18-24 2025 (driven by concurrent Crypto Week, NOT the perps launch)

historical_instances.volume_or_flow_change: Onshores perpetual-futures volume that previously routed to offshore venues (Binance, Bybit, Hyperliquid); structural depth gain for US-regulated derivatives.

historical_instances.trigger: CFTC sues the State of Minnesota to block its prediction-market felony ban (federal preemption).
historical_instances.description: CFTC files its 6th suit in 7 weeks against a state, suing Minnesota in federal court to block a law (signed by Gov. Walz, effective Aug 1 2026) criminalizing prediction/event-contract operation; the CFTC argues event contracts are 'swaps' under the CEA, preempting state gambling law for CFTC-registered DCMs. Prediction-market jurisdiction event with negligible direct crypto-spot impact (BTC -2.11% over the window is unrelated market drift) — relevant chiefly to Kalshi/Polymarket-linked and prediction-market-as-indicator strategies.

price_impacts.window: May 17-23 2026 (unrelated market drift; negligible direct impact)

historical_instances.volume_or_flow_change: Protects CFTC-registered prediction-market access (Kalshi, Polymarket); no direct crypto-spot flow.

categories.summary: Government bans and enforcement actions are among crypto's most reliable bearish shocks: the announcement triggers a fast, sharp sell-off (often the same day) driven by forced de-risking, exchange delistings, and fear of access loss. The two biggest sub-patterns are China-style sovereign trading/mining bans, which hit the entire market (BTC -8% in 24h up to -40% to -55% peak-to-trough), and US SEC securities enforcement, which clobbers the specifically-named token (XRP fell ~52% in 24h and ~73% over the week, other named alts -15-20%) via exchange delistings while barely moving BTC. Crucially, most of these shocks were retraced within weeks-to-months because the underlying network was unaffected, and by 2024-2026 the pattern inverted: dropping lawsuits and lifting sanctions became bullish catalysts.

archetypes.name: Sovereign Trading / Mining Ban (China model)
archetypes.mechanism: A large nation-state (overwhelmingly China, which at peak hosted >50% of BTC hashrate and a huge share of trading volume) bans banks from servicing crypto, bans exchanges, or bans mining outright. On the other side: leveraged longs and Chinese domestic holders are forced to dump into thin liquidity, and miners must liquidate inventory and relocate, creating mechanical sell-pressure. Buyers who keep losing are those who try to catch the falling knife before the cascade of liquidations completes. Because the bans never actually break the decentralized network, supply that was sold gets re-accumulated and price retraces, so the loser over the full cycle is whoever panic-sold the bottom.
leading_signals: state media or PBOC/NDRC statements signaling tightening
rising funding rates / open interest into the news (fuel for liquidation cascade)
USDT/CNY or local-exchange premium going negative
hashrate concentration in the banning jurisdiction
prior leak/rumor cycle (e.g. Sep 2017 exchange-closure rumors preceded confirmation)
archetypes.typical_lag: immediate (intraday to 24h); cascade completes over 2-5 days
archetypes.typical_duration: acute shock 1-2 weeks; full retrace weeks to a few months
archetypes.typical_magnitude_pct: BTC -8% to -16% in 24h; -30% to -55% peak-to-trough over a multi-week crackdown wave
archetypes.recurrence: Recurred repeatedly 2013-2021 (roughly annual China escalations); structurally exhausted after Sep 2021 'all transactions illegal' because China's market share had already been driven near zero

historical_instances.trigger: People's Bank of China bars financial institutions from handling Bitcoin transactions; BTC China then halts CNY deposits (Dec 18)
historical_instances.description: First major sovereign action. BTC fell from a then-record peak near $1,240 toward ~$550 over the following weeks (roughly -55% peak-to-trough). The Dec 18 BTC China CNY-deposit halt accelerated the decline, but reporting does not support a clean single-day -50% crash on Dec 18; it was a multi-day/multi-week decline tied to several China-related events.
historical_instances.volume_or_flow_change: BTC China (then world's largest exchange) halted CNY deposits Dec 18, draining Chinese liquidity

historical_instances.trigger: PBOC + CSRC joint statement declares ICOs illegal fundraising; days later China orders domestic exchange shutdowns (confirmed ~Sep 11-15)
historical_instances.description: The ICO ban knocked BTC sharply on the day; estimates of the same-day move vary widely (early reports cited ~5%, but a fuller intraday reading is ~16-18% from the low-$4,900s toward ~$4,100), so the day-of figure is treated with low confidence. The subsequent exchange-closure order extended the decline: BTC fell from ~$4,950 (Sep 1) to ~$2,817 by Sep 15, roughly -43% peak-to-trough; ETH fell ~35% in tandem. Fully retraced within ~6 weeks as BTC went on to new highs.
historical_instances.volume_or_flow_change: Chinese exchanges (BTCC, Huobi, OKCoin) wound down domestic CNY trading, shifting volume offshore

historical_instances.trigger: Three Chinese financial associations reiterate ban on financial firms providing crypto services; State Council later flags crackdown on mining and trading (May 21)
historical_instances.description: Triggered the largest leverage-driven liquidation cascade in crypto history at the time. BTC fell from ~$58k (May 12) to a ~$36k low (May 19) and ~$31-34k by May 23; ETH fell from ~$4,308 toward ~$2,200. May 19 alone saw a violent intraday flash crash with billions in liquidations.
historical_instances.volume_or_flow_change: Record perp liquidations on May 19 (~$8-9B reported across venues); open interest collapsed

historical_instances.trigger: China intensifies mining crackdown; Sichuan and other provinces order miners to shut down, accelerating the great mining migration
historical_instances.description: BTC fell ~10% intraday from ~$34k toward ~$30,560 and broke below $32k for the first time since early June. Hashrate dropped sharply as Chinese miners went offline; difficulty later posted record negative adjustments.
historical_instances.volume_or_flow_change: Global hashrate fell ~50% over following weeks; largest BTC mining-difficulty downward adjustment on record at the time

historical_instances.trigger: PBOC and ten agencies declare ALL cryptocurrency-related transactions illegal
historical_instances.description: The 'final' China ban. Despite the sweeping language, global BTC fell only ~8% (close-to-close, roughly $44k to $40k) and recovered within days — because China's market share had already been throttled to near zero by the 2017 and 2021 actions, so the marginal impact was modest. BTC went on to its all-time high (~$69k) about six weeks later. Intraday peak-to-trough (~9%) is plausible but not precisely corroborated.
historical_instances.volume_or_flow_change: Exchanges (Huobi, Binance) restricted Chinese users; effect muted by prior offshore migration

historical_instances.trigger: South Korea justice minister says the government is preparing a bill to ban crypto exchanges
historical_instances.description: Korea was a major trading hub (the 'kimchi premium' era). BTC fell as much as ~12% intraday before paring losses as officials walked back the comments. Demonstrates the sovereign-ban template applied to a non-China hub.
historical_instances.volume_or_flow_change: Korean exchanges (Bithumb, Upbit) saw heavy sell-side flow; kimchi premium compressed

archetypes.name: US Securities Enforcement (SEC vs token / exchange)
archetypes.mechanism: The SEC (acutely in the Gensler era) sues an issuer or exchange and names specific tokens as unregistered securities. The mechanism is delisting-driven, not network-driven: US-regulated venues (Coinbase, Kraken, Bittrex, eToro) preemptively suspend the named token to avoid liability, which destroys on-venue liquidity and forces holders to exit before delisting deadlines. On the other side are market makers pulling quotes and US retail dumping into the delisting window. The named token gets crushed; BTC barely moves. Because the legal claim is contestable, the loser over the full cycle is whoever sold the litigation low — XRP, SOL, and others violently re-rated upward on favorable rulings and the 2025 case dismissals.
leading_signals: Wells notice disclosure (precedes suit by weeks)
SEC press-release calendar / enforcement cadence
exchange listing-risk language in filings
a token appearing on a prior SEC complaint's named-securities list (read-across to peers)
options skew / put demand on COIN equity
archetypes.typical_lag: immediate on named token (intraday); delisting-driven leg over days as venues announce suspensions
archetypes.typical_duration: named-token weakness persists weeks-months while litigation unresolved; sharp reversal on favorable ruling
archetypes.typical_magnitude_pct: named token -15% to -25% (alt-L1s) up to -50% to -73% (XRP, due to broad delisting); BTC roughly flat to -7%
archetypes.recurrence: Clustered 2020-2023 (Ripple Dec 2020; Coinbase/Binance June 2023); reversed in 2024-2025 as cases settled or were dropped

historical_instances.trigger: SEC sues Ripple Labs and executives, alleging XRP was a $1.3B unregistered securities offering
historical_instances.description: The archetype's clearest case. XRP fell ~52% in the 24 hours after the suit and roughly -73% over the following week as Coinbase, Bittrex, Crypto.com, OKCoin and others announced suspensions/delistings. BTC was unaffected (in a bull run). XRP later re-rated massively on the July 2023 partial win and the 2025 case resolution. (Earlier catalog figures of -37%/-60% understated the move.)
historical_instances.volume_or_flow_change: Coinbase suspended XRP trading Jan 19 2021; multiple US/global venues delisted, collapsing on-venue liquidity

historical_instances.trigger: SEC sues Binance (June 5) and Coinbase (June 6), naming ~12-13 tokens (SOL, ADA, MATIC, FIL, SAND, AXS, etc.) as securities
historical_instances.description: Hit the named alts hardest while BTC was relatively resilient. SOL fell ~17%, ADA ~15%, MATIC ~15% in the days after; BNB fell ~7%; COIN equity fell ~13%; BTC ~7%. The 'named as a security' list created direct read-across selling to any token on it. Both cases later collapsed/were dismissed in 2024-2025. (Exact alt percentages widely reported but not independently re-derived here, so confidence is medium.)
historical_instances.volume_or_flow_change: US-listing risk spiked; Robinhood later delisted SOL/ADA/MATIC (June 2023), removing US retail venue access

historical_instances.trigger: Coinbase discloses SEC Wells notice (leading indicator of the June 2023 suit)
historical_instances.description: Example of the Wells-notice leading signal. COIN equity and crypto sentiment softened on the disclosure, weeks ahead of the actual lawsuit, illustrating that the enforcement event is often pre-telegraphed and tradable before the headline. The specific -8% next-day move is an unverified estimate.
historical_instances.volume_or_flow_change: Heightened put demand on COIN equity ahead of suit

archetypes.name: National Tax / Access Throttling (India / Nigeria model)
archetypes.mechanism: A government doesn't ban crypto outright but throttles it via punitive taxes (India's 30% gains tax + 1% transaction TDS) or by cutting off banking/internet access to exchanges (Nigeria blocking exchange URLs, detaining Binance staff; RBI's 2018 banking ban). The mechanism is a local-liquidity drain rather than a global price shock: domestic volumes collapse and activity migrates to P2P/offshore/grey markets. Because the throttle is jurisdiction-specific, global BTC price is largely unaffected; the loser is the local exchange ecosystem and local traders, while activity simply reroutes (e.g. Nigerian P2P volume surged after the 2024 crackdown).
affected_scope: the named coin only on local venues
leading_signals: budget-speech / finance-ministry calendar
central-bank banking-access circulars (RBI-style)
local USDT/fiat premium widening or going negative
domestic exchange volume falling vs offshore
P2P volume migration
archetypes.typical_lag: local volume reaction immediate-to-days; structural volume decline plays out over weeks once tax/access is effective
archetypes.typical_duration: persistent — local volume often does not recover (structural, not a dip)
archetypes.typical_magnitude_pct: local exchange volume -70% to -82% (India TDS); local price gap ~-13-17% intraday on announcement; global BTC ~0%
archetypes.recurrence: Recurring policy-driven events in emerging markets (India 2018/2021/2022, Nigeria 2021/2024); each is largely contained to its jurisdiction

historical_instances.trigger: Indian government lists 'The Cryptocurrency and Regulation of Official Digital Currency Bill, 2021' to ban private cryptocurrencies
historical_instances.description: Local-amplified shock. On Indian venues like WazirX, BTC dumped ~15-17% intraday (some prints showed ~-14.8% in two hours); DOGE and SHIB fell >15%. The move was sharply amplified on Indian platforms; global venues also dipped that day (a broad sell-off was underway) but far less than Indian prints, illustrating the jurisdiction-amplified mechanism rather than a clean global-flat decoupling.
historical_instances.volume_or_flow_change: Mass sell-off concentrated on Indian exchanges; global price fell far less

historical_instances.trigger: India's 1% TDS on crypto transfers takes effect (announced Feb 1 2022 budget alongside 30% gains tax)
historical_instances.description: The throttle worked exactly as designed: Indian exchange volumes collapsed ~70% across the board within days, WazirX volume fell ~82% (from $14.53M on June 30 to $3.65M on July 4), ZebPay ~76%, CoinDCX ~70%. A structural, persistent liquidity drain rather than a price dip — global BTC was unaffected.
historical_instances.volume_or_flow_change: WazirX volume -82%, ZebPay -76%, CoinDCX -70% within first week of TDS; ~83% of surveyed traders said they reduced trading frequency

historical_instances.trigger: Nigeria blocks access to crypto exchange websites (Binance, Coinbase, etc.) and detains two Binance executives, blaming crypto for naira depreciation
historical_instances.description: Access-throttle variant. Binance removed NGN trading pairs and converted NGN balances to USDT, disrupting the on-platform naira/USDT market. Rather than killing volume, activity migrated to P2P channels (reports cited large surges in Africa P2P usage). Demonstrates the 'activity reroutes, it doesn't disappear' property of throttles. The +2,228% / +387% figures are single-source reports and not independently verified.
historical_instances.volume_or_flow_change: Binance removed NGN pairs; Africa P2P usage reportedly surged sharply as activity migrated off centralized NGN markets (single-source figures)

archetypes.name: Deregulation / Crackdown Reversal (the inverse, 2024-2026)
archetypes.mechanism: The mirror image of the crackdown archetype: when a previously-imposed ban, sanction, or lawsuit is lifted or dropped, the overhang that suppressed a token clears and price re-rates upward. The mechanism is the removal of forced sellers and the return of access — US exchanges can relist, the litigation discount unwinds, and sidelined capital re-enters. On the other side are shorts and pessimists who had priced in permanent regulatory damage. This pattern became dominant in 2025-2026 as US policy shifted pro-crypto (SEC dropping Coinbase/Ripple actions, Treasury lifting Tornado Cash sanctions), inverting the category's historical bearish bias.
leading_signals: court rulings against the regulator (e.g. Fifth Circuit Tornado Cash ruling)
change in administration / SEC chair (enforcement-cadence regime change)
exchange relisting announcements
settlement / dismissal headlines
litigation-discount narrowing in token's price vs peers
archetypes.typical_lag: immediate on the named token; broader regime re-rating over weeks-months
archetypes.typical_duration: can be durable (regime change) rather than a transient pop
archetypes.typical_magnitude_pct: named token +40% to +100%+ on full relief (e.g. TORN ~+60% on the day); broad sentiment tailwind
archetypes.recurrence: Episodic, tied to legal milestones and political regime change; clustered 2024-2026

historical_instances.trigger: US Treasury OFAC lifts sanctions on Tornado Cash after Nov 2024 Fifth Circuit ruling that immutable smart contracts are not sanctionable 'property'
historical_instances.description: Clear reversal-as-bullish-catalyst. TORN rose ~60% (from roughly $10.20 to ~$16.30 intraday) as the sanctions overhang that had crushed the privacy-mixer token cleared. (Criminal charges against the developers remained pending, which capped the upside.) The earlier catalog figure of +88% / 'doubled from $8 to $15' was overstated.
historical_instances.volume_or_flow_change: TORN trading activity and protocol usage rebounded after delisting reversal

historical_instances.trigger: SEC agrees in principle to dismiss its enforcement lawsuit against Coinbase under the new (Trump-era, post-Gensler) leadership; formal stipulation followed Feb 27 2025
historical_instances.description: Removal of the June-2023 enforcement overhang on the largest US exchange. Marked the regime inversion: the same agency that drove the 2023 crackdown began unwinding it, removing the litigation discount on COIN and on tokens previously named as securities (SOL, ADA, MATIC), which could be relisted on US venues without the security-status cloud. The +5% COIN move is an unverified estimate.
historical_instances.volume_or_flow_change: Removed US-listing/securities overhang on previously-named tokens

categories.summary: US spot crypto ETFs route traditional brokerage money into BTC and ETH; the daily net flow figure (published by Farside/SoSoValue) has become a primary demand gauge. Three repeatable patterns emerge: ETF launch days tend to "sell the news" (a known catalyst is bought into and dumped on the event), sustained multi-week net inflows are reliably bullish while sustained net outflows are bearish (ETFs are a marginal-buyer proxy), and one-time structural outflows from a converted high-fee legacy vehicle (GBTC, ETHE) create mechanical sell pressure independent of sentiment. Flow direction leads or coincides with spot price, making it a strong, computable backtest feature.

archetypes.name: Spot ETF launch day / approval 'sell the news'
archetypes.mechanism: A spot ETF launch is a heavily pre-announced, date-certain catalyst. Traders accumulate spot in the weeks before, pricing in the expected demand. On the actual launch the catalyst is realized, anticipatory longs and arbitrage/event traders take profit, and day-one ETF inflows (while large in headline trading volume) are partly offset by churn and by sellers of the legacy converted trust. Net result is a short-lived local top followed by a 1-2 week drawdown. The buyers who 'lose' are the late-cycle longs who bought the rumor at the highs and the retail crowd chasing the launch headline; the sellers are early accumulators and arbitrageurs unwinding the now-closed discount.
archetypes.typical_lag: immediate to hours; full drawdown plays out over days
archetypes.typical_duration: 1-2 weeks to the local trough, then recovery
archetypes.typical_magnitude_pct: -7% to -20% on the launching asset over 1-2 weeks
archetypes.recurrence: fires once per major product launch (BTC Jan 2024, ETH Jul 2024, HK Apr 2024); a recurring template for every future first-of-kind spot ETF launch (e.g. SOL, XRP)
leading_signals: asset rallies 20-60% in the weeks INTO the approval/launch date
perpetual funding rates elevated / positive heading into launch
options skew bid for calls into the event date
legacy trust (GBTC/ETHE) discount to NAV has collapsed toward zero pre-launch (arbitrage primed to unwind)

historical_instances.trigger: Zcash (ZEC) +180% as the Grayscale Zcash Trust files to convert to a spot ETF and Multicoin discloses a large position.
historical_instances.description: ZEC surged ~+180% over 3 months (Mar 4 $222 -> Jun 4 $624; intra-window peak ~$670, +240% from the low) on the Grayscale Zcash Trust's S-3 filing to convert into a spot ETF plus Multicoin Capital disclosing a large position (+40% in 24h to ~$607). The pump came DESPITE a severe fundamental negative: the entire Electric Coin Co. team (CEO Josh Swihart) resigned in Jan 2026 over a governance battle. A clean case of ETF-conversion-anticipation + institutional accumulation overriding a leaderless-dev situation - the filing/flow bid dominates idiosyncratic fundamentals. (ETF not yet approved; this is the anticipation phase, with sell-the-news risk on the eventual decision.)
historical_instances.volume_or_flow_change: Grayscale Zcash Trust S-3 ETF-conversion filing; Multicoin large position; ECC team walkout (Jan 2026) ignored by the bid.

historical_instances.trigger: US spot Solana ETFs launch (3rd crypto after BTC/ETH) - SOL then crashes ~57% over 4 months.
historical_instances.description: US spot SOL ETFs began trading Oct 28 2025 (Bitwise, Grayscale, VanEck, Fidelity, Franklin, 21Shares, Canary) - the 3rd asset after BTC/ETH to get a US spot ETF. Sell-the-news on the launch (SOL -3.5% over Oct 26-31), then a brutal -57.5% over the next 4 months ($198->$84 by late Feb 2026, low $77.7, -61% max DD) as memecoin volume collapsed and TVL fell 56%. Emphatic confirmation (like ETH's ETF) that a spot-ETF launch is NOT a price floor - structural access ≠ demand when the use-case fades. Cumulative SOL-ETF inflows passed ~$1.12B but couldn't offset the fundamental deterioration.
historical_instances.volume_or_flow_change: Cumulative SOL-ETF inflows ~$1.12B; SOL still crashed ~57% (memecoin volume collapse, TVL -56%).

historical_instances.trigger: First US spot Ethereum ETFs (ETHA, FETH, ETHE conversion, etc.) begin trading; >$1B day-one volume.
historical_instances.description: ETH sold off after launch as ETHE (Grayscale) outflows dominated early net flows and anticipatory longs exited. The decline was sharply compounded by the broad Aug 5 2024 yen carry-trade unwind crash, so the multi-week figure is not purely ETF-attributable.

price_impacts.window: 2024-07-22 close $3,449 to 2024-08-05 close $2,227 (incl. Aug 5 carry-trade crash)

historical_instances.volume_or_flow_change: >$1B launch-day ETF volume; ETHE conversion outflows dominated early net flows

historical_instances.trigger: Asia's first spot BTC and ETH ETFs (China AMC, Bosera HashKey, Harvest) debut on HKEX with in-kind creation.
historical_instances.description: Hugely hyped but volumes disappointed badly (~$300-350M total AUM, <$14M first-week inflows vs billions in the US). No meaningful price catalyst; demonstrates that an ETF launch with weak real flows produces no sustained move.
historical_instances.volume_or_flow_change: ~$284M BTC ETF AUM + ~$66M ETH ETF AUM; <$14M first-week inflows — far below US

historical_instances.trigger: First 10-11 US spot Bitcoin ETFs (IBIT, FBTC, GBTC conversion, ARKB, etc.) begin trading; SEC approved Jan 10. ~$4.6B first-day trading volume.
historical_instances.description: Classic sell-the-news: BTC had rallied into the approval, peaked ~$46-49k, then sold off as the catalyst realized and GBTC outflows began. Local top marked on the event.
historical_instances.volume_or_flow_change: ~$4.6B day-one ETF trading volume; GBTC outflows began immediately (~$5.9B left GBTC by end of Jan)

archetypes.name: Sustained daily net inflows (bullish) / net outflows (bearish)
archetypes.mechanism: Spot ETFs are a structural marginal buyer: every dollar of net inflow forces the authorized participant to buy spot BTC/ETH for creation, and every dollar of net outflow forces a redemption sale. Because ETF demand comes from RIAs, wealth platforms and 401k-adjacent money that does not trade intraday, multi-day/multi-week flow streaks are persistent and trend-reinforcing. Sustained inflows act as a dry-powder injection that lifts price; sustained outflows remove the marginal bid and amplify spot weakness. The losers in an outflow regime are leveraged longs who get liquidated as the structural bid vanishes; winners are those who read the flow tape early. Flow is reflexive — falling price triggers redemptions which push price lower.
archetypes.typical_lag: same-day to 1-3 days (flow tends to lead/coincide with price)
archetypes.typical_duration: as long as the streak persists — days to several weeks
archetypes.typical_magnitude_pct: inflow streaks: +8% to +50% over the run; outflow streaks: -3% to -20% over the run
archetypes.recurrence: ongoing and continuous — flows are reported every trading day; multiple distinct streaks per year
leading_signals: consecutive-day net flow streak (sign and length)
single-day flow magnitude crossing prior records (>$1B in / >$500M out)
IBIT (BTC) / ETHA (ETH) flow dominance — the bellwether funds
rolling 5-day cumulative flow zscore
flow divergence from price (flows turning while price flat = leading signal)

historical_instances.trigger: Spot ETH ETFs post a 10-day inflow streak (longest since the Jul-2024 launch) - a modest mechanical floor.
historical_instances.description: US spot ETH ETFs logged 10 consecutive inflow days through Apr 22 2026, the longest since launch, but MODEST in size (~$50-65M/day total; ETHA $53.6M, FETH $40.6M on the final day) - a mechanical price floor absorbing sell pressure, not 'explosive demand'. ETH bounced +9.41% over the streak. The bid did not reverse the trend: ETH still fell ~40% YTD (to ~$1,811 by Jun 4 2026) with ~$401M of ETH-ETF outflows in May. Lesson: ETF inflow streaks are a floor, not a driver, in a risk-off regime. (Distinct from the Aug-2025 record, which was a $2.85B WEEKLY inflow / $1.02B single-day, not a single-day $2.85B.)

price_impacts.window: Jan 2-Jun 4 2026 ($3,000->$1,811); inflows did not stop the slide

historical_instances.volume_or_flow_change: ~$50-65M/day inflows (BlackRock ETHA/Fidelity FETH led); ~$401M ETH-ETF outflows in May 2026.

historical_instances.trigger: US spot BTC ETFs post an 8-day, $2.1B net-inflow streak (longest since the Oct-2025 9-day run).
historical_instances.description: An 8-day spot-BTC-ETF inflow streak totaling ~$2.1B (Apr 14-23 2026), part of April's $2.44B month (strongest of 2026, BlackRock ~70% of flows). Demand-supportive but modest: BTC rose +3.82% ($74.5k->$77.4k) — an inflow-driven bounce inside a broader 2026 downtrend, not an explosive move.
historical_instances.volume_or_flow_change: ~$2.1B over 8 days; April total $2.44B net inflow.

historical_instances.description: Record ETH ETF inflow day coincided with ETH ripping from ~$3,500 to ~$4,760. Best single example of inflow-driven momentum in ETH.

historical_instances.description: Structural bid removed during macro risk-off; BTC slid from the high-$90k range to the high-$70k/low-$80k range. Confirms sustained outflows are bearish. (Note: original catalog had a Feb 24 2024 date typo and wrong start price; corrected to 2025 with verified prices.)

historical_instances.description: Post-election dry-powder surge. Record inflows coincided with BTC's breakout toward $90k. Strongest demonstration that record inflow days mark accelerating uptrends.
historical_instances.volume_or_flow_change: Record $1.38B single-day net inflow; IBIT $1.11B in one day

archetypes.name: Converted legacy trust structural outflow (GBTC / ETHE)
archetypes.mechanism: When a high-fee closed-end trust (GBTC at 1.5%, ETHE at 2.5%) converts to an open-ended ETF, two mechanical sell forces fire: (1) arbitrageurs who bought the trust at a deep discount to NAV finally redeem for the now-realizable arbitrage profit, and (2) holders rotate to the new low-fee ETFs (IBIT/ETHA at ~0.2%). Each redemption forces the trust to sell underlying spot BTC/ETH on the market. This is supply hitting the market that is independent of sentiment — it is a fee-and-arbitrage-driven, mechanical, front-loaded outflow that decays over weeks as the discount-buyers are flushed out. The 'losers' are spot holders who eat the sell pressure; the trust holders cashing out are realizing a long-awaited arbitrage.
archetypes.typical_lag: begins day 1 of conversion; heaviest in first 2-4 weeks
archetypes.typical_duration: front-loaded over ~6-10 weeks then decays to equilibrium
archetypes.typical_magnitude_pct: contributed -15% to -20% peak-to-trough on BTC over the Jan-Feb 2024 episode; effect fades as outflows slow
archetypes.recurrence: once per converted trust (GBTC Jan 2024, ETHE Jul 2024); a template for any future closed-end-to-ETF conversion
leading_signals: trust discount-to-NAV collapsing toward 0% pre-conversion (signals imminent arbitrage unwind)
trust fee far above competing ETFs (fee gap drives rotation)
daily trust outflow magnitude and its decay rate (slowing = exhaustion / bottoming signal)
trust AUM as % of total category AUM

historical_instances.trigger: GBTC converts to spot ETF; ~$5.9B left GBTC in January alone, heavy daily forced selling.
historical_instances.description: The dominant bearish force in the post-launch correction. Arbitrageurs who bought GBTC at deep discounts redeemed en masse; BTC fell from ~$49k to ~$39.5k. Cumulative GBTC outflows reached ~$14.5B by March 2024 before slowing toward equilibrium by April.
historical_instances.volume_or_flow_change: ~$5.9B GBTC outflows in Jan 2024; ~$14.5B cumulative by March 2024; outflows slowed sharply by April 2024

historical_instances.description: ETHE redemptions were the main reason aggregate ETH ETF net flows ran negative for weeks despite strong inflows into ETHA/FETH, capping ETH's launch. The deeper decline into Aug 5 was amplified by the broad yen carry-trade unwind crash, not by ETHE outflows alone.

categories.summary: Stablecoins are crypto tokens meant to hold a steady $1 value; when they "depeg" (fall below $1) it signals that holders no longer trust the backing, and they rush to redeem or sell. The pattern splits into three repeatable shapes: algorithmic coins (UST/LUNA, IRON/TITAN, deUSD) that have no hard reserves and spiral toward zero, dragging the whole market down; fiat- or asset-backed coins (USDC, USDR, FDUSD) that wobble when their reserves are questioned but usually recover once redemptions resume; and venue-specific flash wicks (USDe, USDT) where thin liquidity on one exchange causes a brief crash that quickly reverts. The biggest event (Terra, May 2022) cascaded into lending desks and exchanges and helped wipe out hundreds of billions across the crypto market.

archetypes.name: Algorithmic / reflexive death-spiral depeg
archetypes.mechanism: An uncollateralized or under-collateralized stablecoin maintains its peg via an arbitrage loop that mints/burns a sister governance/seigniorage token (LUNA for UST, TITAN for IRON, xUSD/deUSD backing). When the peg breaks, arbitrageurs redeem the stablecoin for newly-minted sister tokens and dump them, which crashes the sister token, which forces even more minting to absorb redemptions — a reflexive doom loop. The other side is late holders and yield farmers (e.g. Anchor's 20% APY depositors) who keep holding into the spiral; they lose ~100% because there are no real reserves and supply hyperinflates. There is no natural floor, so these go terminal (to ~zero) rather than recover.
leading_signals: unsustainably high fixed yield (Anchor 19.5%, IRON farms hundreds of percent)
large single-address withdrawals from the anchor pool/farm
Curve/AMM pool tilting heavily to the stablecoin side
stablecoin trading 0.98-0.99 persistently before the break
concentration of backing in one risky counterparty (Stream Finance)
sister-token price rolling over while stablecoin still ~pegged
archetypes.typical_lag: immediate to hours once the peg cracks; full collapse over 1-7 days
archetypes.typical_duration: terminal — days to a week to reach near-zero; no recovery
archetypes.typical_magnitude_pct: -50% to -100% on the stablecoin; -95% to -100% on the sister token
archetypes.recurrence: recurring; ~once every 6-18 months a notable algo design fails (2021 IRON, 2022 UST/USDD, 2025 xUSD/deUSD)

historical_instances.trigger: UST broke peg after large Anchor withdrawals and coordinated Curve 4pool/UST selling; reflexive LUNA minting hyperinflated supply into the trillions
historical_instances.description: Terra UST/LUNA collapse — the canonical algorithmic death spiral. UST fell from $1 to roughly $0.02 by May 13; LUNA from ~$80+ (early May) to effectively zero by May 13. Helped wipe tens of billions in a week and triggered the 2022 contagion (3AC, Celsius, Voyager).
historical_instances.volume_or_flow_change: LFG sold tens of thousands of BTC from reserves trying to defend the peg; record Curve/exchange UST sell volume; broad multi-month crypto value destruction

historical_instances.trigger: Large TITAN holders sold near the peak (~$60-65), IRON broke peg below $0.75, triggering crypto's first large-scale DeFi bank run; redemption mechanism minted unlimited TITAN
historical_instances.description: IRON Finance / TITAN collapse on Polygon. IRON (partial-algo, 75% USDC + 25% TITAN) depegged to below $0.75; TITAN went from ~$60-65 to near $0 within hours. Mark Cuban was a notable LP.
historical_instances.volume_or_flow_change: Mass redemption/panic selling; TITAN supply hyperinflated to quadrillions

historical_instances.trigger: Post-Terra fear spread to Tron's algorithmic USDD; coordinated selling pushed it off peg
historical_instances.description: USDD (Tron, algo, backed by TRX/BTC/USDC standby reserve) depegged ~9% to around $0.91. Justin Sun / TronDAO deployed reserves to defend; TRX fell sharply during the episode. Peg partially restored but USDD traded below $1 for months.
historical_instances.volume_or_flow_change: TronDAO reserve deployed (USDC injection + TRX moved off Binance) to defend the peg

historical_instances.trigger: Stream Finance disclosed a ~$93M loss (external fund manager misappropriation / liquidations stemming from the Oct 10 crash); xUSD backing evaporated and withdrawals were frozen
historical_instances.description: Stream Finance xUSD collapse + Elixir deUSD contagion. xUSD fell to roughly $0.26 (some reports of a deeper ~$0.18 low later). deUSD, heavily exposed to Stream via private lending/vault positions, collapsed ~98% from ~$1.00 to ~$0.015 after Stream defaulted and Elixir halted redemptions. Revolving-loan leverage amplified a $93M hole into hundreds of millions of bad debt across Morpho, Euler, Silo and related protocols.
historical_instances.volume_or_flow_change: Hundreds of millions in debt exposure across lending protocols; Elixir wound down deUSD

archetypes.name: Fiat/asset-backed reserve-shock depeg
archetypes.mechanism: A collateralized stablecoin (USDC, USDR, FDUSD) loses peg because the market doubts the reserves are fully redeemable — a real impairment (USDC's $3.3B stuck at failed SVB; USDR's treasury drained of liquid DAI) or a credible accusation (Justin Sun calling FDUSD's custodian insolvent). Redemptions queue while secondary-market sellers dump below $1 to exit immediately; the discount equals the market's probability-weighted haircut on the reserve. The other side is arbitrageurs/redeemers who buy the discounted coin betting reserves are good and 1:1 redemption resumes — they win when backing is real (USDC recovered to $1 in ~3 days) and lose when it's genuinely gone (USDR ~half permanent). Key read-across: when the trigger is TradFi banking stress (SVB), BTC RALLIES as a banking-system hedge.
affected_scope: stablecoins sharing its collateral (DAI, FRAX held USDC)
leading_signals: disclosure of reserve held at a stressed bank/custodian
issuer pausing or slowing redemptions
prominent insolvency accusation against the issuer
collateral concentration in illiquid/RWA assets vs cash
downstream protocols (Maker, Frax) carrying large balances of the coin
archetypes.typical_lag: immediate (minutes-hours of disclosure); worst during banking off-hours / weekends
archetypes.typical_duration: hours to days; full recovery if reserves are real, permanent partial loss if not
archetypes.typical_magnitude_pct: -9% to -25% for credible fiat-backed (recovers); ~-49% for impaired RWA-backed (permanent)
archetypes.recurrence: recurring; clustered around banking/custodian stress events

historical_instances.trigger: Resolv (USR) depegs ~80% after an AWS-KMS private-key compromise mints $80M unbacked USR.
historical_instances.description: An attacker compromised Resolv's AWS KMS environment to obtain the privileged off-chain signing key and mint ~$80M of unbacked USR (the smart contract enforced no mint cap, only a valid signature). USR depegged from $1 to a low of ~$0.025 (-97%; measured -79.8% close-to-close over Mar 20-26, $0.999->$0.20), settling ~$0.27-0.40; the attacker converted USR->wstUSR->stablecoins->ETH, extracting ~$25M. Resolv halted operations. A backing-destroyed depeg caused by key-management failure (cf. the infinite-mint bridge exploits) - the peg breaks the instant unbacked supply exceeds reserves.
historical_instances.volume_or_flow_change: $80M unbacked USR minted; ~$25M extracted in ETH; protocol halted.

historical_instances.trigger: Tron founder Justin Sun publicly accused FDUSD custodian First Digital Trust of being insolvent / having mishandled client funds
historical_instances.description: FDUSD depeg on insolvency accusation. FDUSD fell sharply intraday — reported lows range from ~$0.87 (~-13%) to ~$0.76 (~-24%) depending on venue/snapshot. First Digital denied the claims, processed redemptions, and sued Sun for defamation. Peg recovered. The bulk of FDUSD supply traded on Binance, concentrating the panic.
historical_instances.volume_or_flow_change: First Digital processed redemptions to defend peg; concentrated Binance selling

historical_instances.trigger: USDR (Tangible) treasury drained of liquid DAI by redemptions; most backing was illiquid tokenized real estate + TNGBL insurance fund
historical_instances.description: USDR real-estate-backed stablecoin collapse. Dropped to ~$0.50-0.51 (down ~49-50%) within hours once the DAI liquidity buffer ran out; some reports note a deeper drop later in the week. Permanent partial loss — illiquid RWA backing could not meet redemptions. Issuer outlined a wind-down/recovery via the underlying property assets.

historical_instances.trigger: Circle disclosed ~$3.3B of USDC reserves stuck at the failed Silicon Valley Bank; depeg worsened over the weekend with banking closed

archetypes.name: Venue-specific liquidity-cascade flash depeg
archetypes.mechanism: A well-collateralized stablecoin briefly prints a sharp discount on ONE venue (a single exchange order book or an imbalanced AMM pool) because of a localized liquidity vacuum — forced liquidations dump collateral into thin books, or an oracle/pricing quirk triggers a cascade, while the coin stays at ~$1 everywhere else and remains fully redeemable. The other side is fast arbitrageurs who buy the venue wick and redeem/sell elsewhere at par; they win because the discount is mechanical, not fundamental. These revert within minutes-to-hours and are NOT true insolvency events, but the wick itself can auto-liquidate over-collateralized borrowers who used the coin as collateral, propagating real losses.
leading_signals: market-wide forced-liquidation cascade in progress (Oct 10-11 2025)
single-venue order-book depth collapsing
AMM stable-pool weight skewing past ~70/30
high leverage / open interest using the stablecoin as margin
thin weekend/off-hours liquidity
archetypes.typical_lag: instantaneous wick; reverts in minutes to a few hours
archetypes.typical_duration: minutes to hours; usually fully recovered same day
archetypes.typical_magnitude_pct: -1% to -35% on the affected venue only; ~0% on aggregate/redeemable price
archetypes.recurrence: frequent; minor venue wicks happen several times a year, severe ones during big liquidation cascades

historical_instances.trigger: Market-wide forced-liquidation cascade (one of the largest in crypto history); on Binance, USDe collateral was dumped into thin books causing a localized wick
historical_instances.description: USDe (Ethena) Oct 2025 flash depeg. USDe wicked to roughly $0.65 on Binance during the crash, while remaining ~$0.99 on Curve/Uniswap and fully redeemable/overcollateralized — a venue-specific dislocation, not a global depeg. ENA cratered on contagion fear; USDe supply later contracted as confidence fell. Aggregate CoinGecko data shows only a -0.88% max drawdown over the window, confirming the wick was venue-isolated.
historical_instances.volume_or_flow_change: Large market-wide liquidations; USDe supply and outflows fell materially over the following month

categories.summary: Stablecoins (mainly USDT and USDC) are the dollar on-ramp for crypto, so changes in how many of them exist act as a proxy for buying power entering or leaving the market. When issuers mint new coins, that "dry powder" tends to precede or accompany rallies; when holders redeem coins and supply shrinks, it tends to mark deleveraging and weak demand. Sudden doubts about whether a stablecoin is actually backed (a depeg) trigger panic-selling and a flight to safety, briefly crashing the whole market.

archetypes.name: Net Mint Expansion (Dry-Powder Injection)
archetypes.mechanism: Tether and Circle mint new stablecoins on-demand when authorized resellers and exchanges wire in USD (or pre-mint inventory in anticipation of demand). Newly issued USDT/USDC lands on exchange order books and becomes immediate quote-currency buying power. The counterparty is whoever sells crypto into that fresh bid; structurally, rising net supply reflects net USD inflow into the ecosystem, which is a leading-to-coincident demand signal. The reflexive part: traders watch Whale Alert mint notifications and front-run expected inflows, so price can react within minutes on sentiment alone even before the coins are deployed.
leading_signals: Tether Treasury / Circle mint transactions on-chain (Whale Alert tags)
30-day net change in aggregate stablecoin market cap turning positive / accelerating
USDT minted to Tron/Ethereum 'authorized but not issued' inventory then moved to exchanges
rising stablecoin balances on exchange hot wallets (exchange stablecoin reserves up)
stablecoin market-cap making new ATH while BTC consolidates
archetypes.typical_lag: minutes-to-hours on sentiment for large discrete mints; days-to-weeks for the aggregate-supply-growth trend to map onto price
archetypes.typical_duration: discrete mint pop fades within 24-72h; sustained net-supply expansion can fuel multi-week to multi-month uptrends
archetypes.typical_magnitude_pct: +0.4% to +2.5% on BTC within hours of a discrete >=$1B mint; +10% to +300% on BTC over multi-month sustained supply-expansion regimes
archetypes.recurrence: very frequent — discrete $1B+ USDT mints occur dozens of times per year; sustained-expansion regimes align with each major bull leg (2020-21, late-2024, 2025)

historical_instances.trigger: Total stablecoin market cap hits a record ~$322B (exceeds the FX reserves of 95 nations).
historical_instances.description: Total stablecoin supply reached a record ~$318-323B in late May 2026 (USDT ~$189.7B + USDC ~$77.9B = ~85%), exceeding the FX reserves of 95 countries — a structural dry-powder build driven by payments and DeFi/AI-agent demand. IMPORTANT nuance: this dry powder kept EXPANDING while BTC fell ~40% YTD and crashed into June — confirming that aggregate stablecoin supply is a slow structural tailwind, NOT a timing signal for spot direction in a risk-off regime (the dry powder sits idle until risk appetite returns).

archetypes.name: Net Burn / Redemption Contraction
archetypes.mechanism: When holders redeem stablecoins for USD, issuers burn the coins and supply contracts. Shrinking supply means dollars are leaving the crypto ecosystem (or being parked off-exchange), removing quote-currency buying power. Burns cluster after corrections as deleveraged traders cash out, so falling supply is a coincident-to-confirming bearish signal: less dry powder, weaker bid. The counterparty narrative reverses — there's progressively less new money to absorb sell pressure.
leading_signals: 30-day net change in aggregate stablecoin market cap turning negative
large USDT/USDC burns on-chain (Treasury redemptions)
exchange stablecoin reserves declining
USDT market cap falling sharply over days (mass redemption)
stablecoin supply rolling over after a parabolic expansion
archetypes.typical_lag: coincident-to-lagging — supply contraction typically follows the price correction by days, confirming weakness rather than leading it
archetypes.typical_duration: weeks-to-months; supply contraction regimes persist through bear phases
archetypes.typical_magnitude_pct: contraction confirms ongoing drawdowns; standalone signal, hard to isolate, but accompanied BTC drawdowns of -30% to -70% in 2022
archetypes.recurrence: occasional regime-level events — major contractions tied to 2022 bear market and acute redemption scares; routine small burns are frequent but low-signal

archetypes.name: Stablecoin Depeg Shock (Backing-Doubt)
archetypes.mechanism: When the market loses confidence that a stablecoin is fully backed (reserves stuck at a failed bank, opaque attestations, or an algorithmic peg breaking), the coin trades below $1. Because stablecoins are core collateral and quote currency, a depeg triggers a scramble: holders dump the suspect coin, DeFi positions backed by it get liquidated, and capital flees to BTC/ETH or fiat. The result is a sharp, fast risk-off shock across the whole market. Counterparties who keep the peg are arbitrageurs who buy below $1 betting on redemption — they win if backing is real, lose catastrophically (UST) if it isn't.
leading_signals: stablecoin secondary-market price deviating >50bps from $1
issuer disclosure of reserve exposure to a stressed counterparty/bank
spiking redemption queue / mint-burn imbalance
Curve/DEX stablecoin pool imbalance (one coin dominating the pool)
rising funding-rate stress and CEX withdrawal spikes
archetypes.typical_lag: immediate — the market reacts within minutes to hours of the depeg headline
archetypes.typical_duration: acute phase 24-72h; full peg recovery hours-to-days if backing is real, permanent if not (UST)
archetypes.typical_magnitude_pct: depegged coin -3% to -100%; BTC/ETH -8% to -16% in the acute window, with sharp V-recoveries when backing is confirmed
archetypes.recurrence: rare but high-impact — a handful of major events (UST May 2022, USDC Mar 2023, USDe Oct 2025); minor wobbles more frequent

archetypes.name: Low SSR / Loaded Exchange Reserves (Positioning Indicator)
archetypes.mechanism: The Stablecoin Supply Ratio (SSR = BTC market cap / stablecoin market cap) measures how much BTC the existing stablecoin pile could buy. A low SSR means stablecoins have outsized buying power relative to BTC — the 'fuel tank' is full — and historically precedes rallies because the dry powder is already sitting on exchanges. The counterparty: shorts and sellers who get overwhelmed when that sidelined stablecoin liquidity deploys. Caveat: a low SSR can also be produced by capital LEAVING (BTC market cap falling faster than stablecoins), which inverts the signal — so it must be read alongside whether stablecoin supply is actually growing.
leading_signals: SSR falling to historically low band (cited ~13-21 range at cycle bottoms)
SSR oscillator below lower Bollinger band
rising exchange stablecoin reserves concurrent with low SSR (confirms accumulation vs outflow)
stablecoin market cap rising while BTC consolidates (SSR compressing for bullish reason)
archetypes.typical_lag: days-to-weeks — a positioning indicator, not a precise timing trigger
archetypes.typical_duration: the bullish deployment phase can run weeks-to-months once dry powder is confirmed loaded
archetypes.typical_magnitude_pct: historically preceded multi-month BTC rebounds of tens of percent when accompanied by genuine stablecoin accumulation
archetypes.recurrence: cyclical — low-SSR readings clustered at the mid-2021, late-2022, and 2024 rebound phases

historical_instances.description: Cautionary low-SSR reading. Demonstrates the failure mode: a low SSR produced by shrinking BTC cap amid outflows is NOT bullish dry powder, illustrating why the signal must be paired with exchange-reserve direction. Forward-looking and unverified.

categories.summary: Technical and on-chain signals are computed triggers — moving-average crosses, leverage/funding extremes, and valuation ratios — that traders watch to time crypto entries and exits. The most backtestable patterns split into two families: lagging trend signals (death/golden cross, 200-day MA loss/reclaim) that confirm moves already underway, and positioning/valuation extremes (funding spikes, open-interest washouts, MVRV Z-score, realized price) that mark exhaustion and tend to mean-revert. In practice the cleanest edges come from forced-liquidation washouts (sharp down, fast bounce) and on-chain undervaluation (MVRV < 1 = multi-month accumulation zone), while MA crosses mostly confirm rather than predict.

archetypes.name: Moving-average regime cross (golden/death cross, 200-DMA loss/reclaim)
archetypes.mechanism: The 50/200-day cross and the 200-day MA are lagging trend filters. They do not cause moves; they confirm a regime that price already entered. The 'other side' is momentum/CTA-style systematic flows that flip exposure on the cross, plus retail that treats the cross as a headline-driven signal. Because the 200-DMA is a widely-watched support/resistance line, it becomes a self-fulfilling cluster of stops and limit orders: losing it triggers stop cascades; reclaiming it triggers breakout buying. The recurring loser is the trader who treats the death cross as a fresh short entry near the local low — historically returns in the first ~3 weeks after a BTC death cross are roughly a coin flip and 2-3 months later average a 15-26% recovery.
archetypes.typical_lag: lagging — the cross prints days-to-weeks AFTER the price move; the 200-DMA break itself reacts within hours
archetypes.typical_duration: regime label persists weeks to months; the acute stop-cascade on a 200-DMA loss plays out in 1-3 days
archetypes.typical_magnitude_pct: 200-DMA loss often accompanies a -10% to -25% drawdown already in progress; reclaim tends to precede +15% to +40% over 1-3 months
archetypes.recurrence: BTC has printed multiple death crosses 2018-2025; 200-DMA tested multiple times per cycle
leading_signals: 50-DMA flattening and converging toward 200-DMA
price closing below 200-DMA on rising volume
50/200 spread narrowing to within 1-2%
200-DMA slope turning negative

archetypes.name: Open-interest washout / forced-liquidation cascade
archetypes.mechanism: When perpetual-futures open interest and leverage build up (often alongside elevated funding), a sharp price move triggers margin calls. Exchange liquidation engines market-sell (or buy) collateral, pushing price further into the same direction, triggering the next tier of liquidations — a reflexive deleveraging cascade. The losers are the over-leveraged longs (in a down-washout) whose stops/liquidations become the fuel; the winners are spot buyers and patient capital who absorb the forced selling at a discount. Because the move is mechanical (positions, not fundamentals), it typically overshoots and snaps back fast — the washout itself is often a short-term bottom.
archetypes.typical_lag: immediate — minutes to hours; the full cascade often completes inside 24h
archetypes.typical_duration: acute crash 1-2 days; OI/leverage reset persists weeks; price often recovers a large fraction within 1-2 weeks
archetypes.typical_magnitude_pct: BTC -8% to -30% in 24h; alts/high-beta -15% to -50%; bounce of +5% to +15% within days
archetypes.recurrence: the largest cascades cluster ~1-2 per year, with the biggest in May 2021, Aug 2024, and Oct 2025
leading_signals: aggregate open interest at cycle highs
funding rate persistently positive/elevated
long/short ratio skewed heavily long
estimated-leverage-ratio rising
thin spot order-book depth into a macro catalyst

archetypes.name: On-chain valuation extreme (MVRV Z-score, realized price, NUPL, Pi-cycle)
archetypes.mechanism: These metrics compare market price to aggregate cost basis (realized value). MVRV Z-score, realized price, NUPL and the Pi-cycle top indicator all measure how far price has stretched from what holders actually paid. At extreme highs (MVRV-Z > ~7, NUPL in euphoria, Pi-cycle crossover) holders are sitting on huge unrealized gains and the marginal buyer is exhausted — the other side is profit-taking long-term holders distributing into euphoric retail. At extreme lows (MVRV ratio < 1, price below realized price) the average holder is underwater — historically a maximum-pain accumulation zone where patient capital buys from capitulating sellers. Mean-reverting and slow: these are cycle markers, not day-trade triggers.
archetypes.typical_lag: slow — signals fire days-to-weeks around tops/bottoms; full reversion plays out over months
archetypes.typical_duration: months; sub-1.0 MVRV windows lasted ~15 days (Mar 2020), ~178 days (2022), ~305 days (2018-19)
archetypes.typical_magnitude_pct: buying sub-1.0 MVRV historically returned triple-digit % within 12 months; selling MVRV-Z>7 avoided 50-80% cycle drawdowns
archetypes.recurrence: once or twice per ~4-year cycle for the full extreme
leading_signals: MVRV Z-score crossing above 7 (top) or MVRV ratio below 1 (bottom)
price crossing below realized price
NUPL entering 'euphoria' (>0.75) or 'capitulation' (<0)
Pi-cycle 111DMA crossing above 2x350DMA
long-term-holder supply distributing

archetypes.name: Funding-rate / leverage-sentiment extreme (contrarian)
archetypes.mechanism: Perpetual funding is paid from the crowded side to the other side. Extreme positive funding (e.g. 0.2-0.3% per 8h sustained for weeks) means longs are paying heavily to stay long — crowded, over-leveraged positioning that signals euphoria and a vulnerable market near local tops. Deeply negative funding means shorts are paying — capitulation/panic positioning that often marks bottoms. The recurring losers are momentum-chasers piling into the crowded side at the extreme; the winners are contrarians who fade the crowd and/or harvest the funding (cash-and-carry). Funding extremes are a sentiment gauge, not precise timing — they identify fragility, with the actual reversal often needing a catalyst (which then becomes an OI washout).
archetypes.typical_lag: days to weeks — extreme funding flags fragility; the reversal follows on a catalyst
archetypes.typical_duration: the positioning unwind resolves over days once triggered; funding normalizes within a week of a washout
archetypes.typical_magnitude_pct: fading sustained extreme-positive funding historically preceded -20% to -50% cycle corrections; buying deeply-negative funding preceded +30% to +600% recoveries
archetypes.recurrence: milder funding spikes several times a year; true multi-week extremes once or twice a cycle
leading_signals: funding rate sustained >0.1-0.3% per 8h for days/weeks (top warning)
funding deeply negative (bottom signal)
OI rising while funding rich
perp basis / annualized basis at extreme
long/short account ratio skewed

historical_instances.trigger: Perpetual funding (e.g. BitMEX XBTUSD) ran elevated, around 0.2-0.3% per 8h at times, into the April 2021 first peak.
historical_instances.description: Sustained elevated-positive funding flagged euphoric over-leveraged longs ahead of the May 2021 crash — a textbook 'too crowded long' top warning, though exact sustained levels vary by source.
